content.content:
Web search results for query: "kustomize edit set image GitHub Actions CI CD update overlay tag SHA 2025"

Links: [{"title":"Automation with GitHub Actions and Kustomize | GAP","url":"https://gap.gjensidige.io/docs/guides/ci-automation-with-kustomize"},{"title":"kustomize edit set image should correctly parse both tag and digest · Issue #4713 · kubernetes-sigs/kustomize","url":"https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/issues/4713"},{"title":"Kustomize Edit image · Actions · GitHub Marketplace · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/marketplace/actions/kustomize-edit-image"},{"title":"Setup Kustomize · Actions · GitHub Marketplace · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/marketplace/actions/setup-kustomize"},{"title":"Building a CI/CD Pipeline for Kubernetes Microservices (2025)","url":"https://atmosly.com/blog/building-a-complete-cicd-pipeline-for-microservices-on-kubernetes-2025"},{"title":"Variable Docker image tags · Issue #51 · kubernetes-sigs/kustomize","url":"https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/issues/51"},{"title":"\"kustomize edit set image\" does not retain image tag information on setting newName · Issue #4375 · kubernetes-sigs/kustomize","url":"https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/issues/4375"},{"title":"End to End LLMOps Pipeline — Part 9 — Kustomize   | Medium","url":"https://devopslearning.medium.com/end-to-end-llmops-pipeline-part-9-kustomize-b96aed413d6c"},{"title":"[Question] Kustomize set image not working in combination with base/overlays setup · Issue #3526 · kubernetes-sigs/kustomize","url":"https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/issues/3526"},{"title":"How to pass a variable through kustomize edit set image command? · Issue #2475 · kubernetes-sigs/kustomize","url":"https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/issues/2475"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about using `kustomize edit set image` with GitHub Actions CI/CD to update overlay tags with SHA values:

## Common Workflow Pattern

A typical GitHub Actions workflow includes a step that uses `kustomize edit set image app-image=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.TEAM_NAME }}/${{ github.event.repository.name }}:${{ github.sha }}` to update deployment manifests.

## Working with Overlays

When using a base/overlay structure in Kustomize, you navigate to the specific overlay directory and run `kustomize edit set image` from there, for example: `cd apps/user-service/overlays/production` followed by `kustomize edit set image user-service=myregistry.com/user-service:v1.2.3`.

## GitHub Actions Integration

The `kustomize edit set image` command updates the kustomization.yaml file to set a new image, with the image name set dynamically using environment variables or secrets provided in a CI/CD pipeline (e.g., GitHub Actions).

## Best Practices

After updating the kustomization file with the new image tag, the changes should be pushed back to the repository to keep it in sync with GitOps practices, and `[skip ci]` should be added to the commit message to avoid re-running the workflow.

## GitOps CI/CD Loop with GitHub Actions, Kustomize, and ArgoCD

The search results reveal current best practices (2025-2026) for implementing a GitOps workflow:

### Core Workflow Pattern

In a GitOps workflow, GitHub Actions handles building & testing, pushing container images, and updating Git manifests, while ArgoCD detects changes and syncs to the cluster.

### Key Components

**GitHub Actions Workflow:**
GitHub Actions builds and pushes Docker images, then updates the config repo using kustomize to set the image tag. Kustomize is used via commands like `kustomize edit set image` to update image tags in manifests.

**Commit Back to Git:**
GitHub Actions commits and pushes changes back to the infrastructure repository with a configured user identity.

**ArgoCD Detection:**
ArgoCD detects the updated image tag in your GitOps repository and syncs the Kubernetes manifests accordingly.

### Benefits of This Pattern

Integrating GitHub Actions with ArgoCD creates a complete GitOps pipeline where code changes flow automatically from pull request to production. This pattern represents the "Gold Standard" for modern Kubernetes-native delivery, offering automation, security, and progressive delivery capabilities.

### Best Practices (2026)

Do not store ArgoCD tokens in application repos - store them in CI secret management only, and Git revert is preferred over ArgoCD rollback because it maintains the GitOps principle that Git is the single source of truth.

I've found comprehensive information about GitHub Actions workflows for pushing commits back to repositories with Kustomize image tag updates. Here are the key findings:

## Workflow Architecture

A typical workflow uses a "Build and Deploy" trigger that includes a "build-and-push" job followed by an "update-manifest" job that uses `actions/checkout@v2` with a custom token, then runs `kustomize edit set image` to update the deployment image tag, and finally commits and pushes changes back to the repository.

## Permissions and Token Configuration

To push commits back to the repository, you need to set the contents-permission of the default GITHUB_TOKEN to true in your workflow permissions, which is required to push new commits to the repository. The `contents: write` permission enables this functionality.

## Image Tag Update Pattern

The workflow updates deployment image tags using `kustomize edit set image app-image=${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.TEAM_NAME }}/${{ github.event.repository.name }}:${{ github.sha }}`, which dynamically sets the image tag based on the Git SHA.

## Best Practices

1. **Preventing Infinite Loops**: Add `[skip ci]` to the commit message to avoid re-running the workflow for commits made by the automation.

2. **GitOps Alignment**: Push the changes back to your code base so that your Infrastructure as Code repository is up to date in accordance with GitOps practices.

3. **Image Tagging Strategy**: Flux recommends using real version numbers in your image tags with canonical ordering, as the alternative of using commit time is racy and doesn't guarantee the latest commit will be deployed.

4. **Handling Infinite Loops**: If using a Personal Access Token to push commits, the resulting push can trigger other GitHub Actions workflows and result in an infinite loop, which can be prevented by adding `skip-checks:true` to the commit message.

I found comprehensive information about avoiding infinite loops in GitHub Actions workflows when committing back to your repository. Here are the key approaches:

## Using `[skip ci]` in Commit Messages

You can skip workflow runs triggered by the push and pull_request events by including a command in your commit message. If any commit message in your push or the HEAD commit of your PR contains the strings [skip ci], [ci skip], [no ci], [skip actions], or [actions skip] workflows triggered on the push or pull_request events will be skipped.

To use this approach, include one of these keywords in your automated commit message:
```yaml
git commit -m "[skip ci] Your commit message here"
```

## Token Considerations

When pushing from GitHub Actions, the type of token you use matters significantly:

- GitHub provides workflows with the GITHUB_TOKEN variable, which is explicitly configured to not run subsequent workflows to avoid infinite loop behavior.
- Using a personal access token avoids this check, and will allow you to get into infinite loops if you're not careful.

## Practical Example

When committing version changes, you can use a commit message like "[skip ci] Bump Version to $NEW_VERSION" to prevent the workflow from re-triggering.

## Additional Protections

For workflows that use Personal Access Tokens or need more control, GitHub Actions skips workflows triggered by the default GITHUB_TOKEN if the commit is made by github-actions[bot], but explicit filtering ensures reliability.

The recommended approach is to always use `[skip ci]` in your commit messages when your workflow pushes automated changes back to the repository.

Based on the search results, here's information about the `docker/metadata-action` SHA prefix format output tags with `type=sha` in GitHub Actions:

## SHA Tag Format

The `type=sha` tag type outputs Git short commit (or long if specified) as Docker tag like sha-860c190. By default, the length of the short commit SHA is 7 characters.

## Customizing SHA Prefix and Length

You can dynamically set the branch name as a prefix using `type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-`, which would generate tags like `branch-name-abc1234`.

You can increase the length of the short commit SHA for larger repositories by setting the DOCKER_METADATA_SHORT_SHA_LENGTH environment variable. For example:

```yaml
- name: Docker meta
  id: meta
  uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
  with:
    images: name/app
    tags: |
      type=sha
  env:
    DOCKER_METADATA_SHORT_SHA_LENGTH: 12
```

## Output Example

An example output with sha tag type generates tags like `name/app:sha-90dd603`.

## Key Concepts

ArgoCD's API server can be configured to receive webhook events from GitHub to eliminate polling delays, as ArgoCD supports Git webhook notifications from GitHub.

## How It Works

With webhooks, the Git server notifies ArgoCD immediately when a push happens. ArgoCD detects changes within seconds, with total time from push to sync start typically under 5 seconds, compared to the default 3-minute polling interval.

## Configuration Steps

The payload URL configured in the Git provider should use the /api/webhook endpoint of your ArgoCD instance (e.g. https://argocd.example.com/api/webhook).

When creating the webhook in GitHub, the "Content type" needs to be set to "application/json".

You can configure the GitHub webhook secret in the argocd-secret Kubernetes secret with the webhook.github.secret key.

## Optimization

You should increase the polling interval since webhooks handle immediate detection, and polling serves as a fallback for webhook delivery failures.

The search results include configuration examples, troubleshooting guidance, and best practices for setting up GitHub webhooks with the argocd-server component to trigger immediate synchronization on push events.

content.content:
# Docker Metadata Action: SHA Output Format & Configuration

## SHA Output Format

The `type=sha` generates tags based on the commit SHA. With `prefix=sha-`, the output format is:
**`sha-<short-sha>`**

For example, with a commit SHA of `860c1904a1ce19322e91ac35af1ab07466440c37`, it produces: `sha-860c190`

## Default SHA Length

"Specifies the length of the short commit SHA to ensure uniqueness. Default is `7`" through the `DOCKER_METADATA_SHORT_SHA_LENGTH` environment variable, though larger repositories may need to increase this for uniqueness.

## Getting the Short SHA Value

The action automatically truncates the full commit hash. You can adjust the length via the environment variable `DOCKER_METADATA_SHORT_SHA_LENGTH` if the default 7 characters don't provide sufficient uniqueness in your repository.

## Action Outputs Provided

The metadata action generates these outputs:

- **`version`** — Docker image version
- **`tags`** — Complete Docker tags
- **`tag-names`** — Tag names without the image base name
- **`labels`** — Docker labels
- **`annotations`** — BuildKit annotations
- **`json`** — JSON representation of tags and labels
- **`bake-file-tags`**, **`bake-file-labels`**, **`bake-file-annotations`** — Bake definition files

Based on the web search results, here's what I found regarding GitHub Actions, GITHUB_TOKEN, push commits, permissions, and infinite loop prevention:

## Infinite Loop Prevention with GITHUB_TOKEN

GitHub executes each Workflow with a token — ${{ github.token }} — that has permissions to push commits back to a repository; however, to prevent infinite Workflow loops, the tasks performed with this token do not trigger further Workflow runs.

More specifically, when you use the repository's GITHUB_TOKEN to perform tasks, events triggered by the GITHUB_TOKEN, with the exception of workflow_dispatch and repository_dispatch, will not create a new workflow run.

## Permissions Configuration

To push commits with GITHUB_TOKEN, you need to set proper permissions. You can give the default GITHUB_TOKEN write permission to commit and push added or changed files to the repository using a permissions block with contents: write.

## When Infinite Loops Can Occur

The key caveat is that if you're using a Personal Access Token (PAT) to push commits to a GitHub repository, the resulting commit or push can trigger other GitHub Actions workflows, which can result in an infinite loop.

## workflow_dispatch Exception

An important note for your scenario: When using GITHUB_TOKEN, events it initiates (except workflow_dispatch and repository_dispatch) will not activate additional workflows, which is helpful to prevent infinite loops. This means workflow_dispatch events created by GITHUB_TOKEN can still trigger workflows.

## Best Practices for Prevention

To prevent the infinite loop caused by commits from your GitHub Action, add a conditional check to skip the workflow when the commit is made by the GitHub Actions bot itself, or filter by actor in the workflow step by checking if github.actor != 'github-actions[bot]'.

Based on the search results, here's information about the `kustomize edit set image` syntax and how to work with `newName` and `newTag` in kustomization.yaml:

## Kustomization.yaml Structure

To change the image name, tag or registry, you declare a special images directive inside the kustomization.yaml with the format: `images: - name: <imageName> newName: <newImageName> newTag: <newImageTag>`

## Example in kustomization.yaml

For example, you could update kustomization.yaml as follows: `images: - name: busybox newName: my-private.docker.registry/alpine newTag: 3.17.3`

Another example: images: - name: my-app newName: new-app-1 newTag: MYNEWTAG-1

## Using kustomize edit set image Command

Declaring images in the kustomization.yaml enables you to use the command `kustomize edit set image app-image=...` in your automation. The kustomize-set-image command updates the kustomization.yaml file in a specified directory to reflect a different revision of a container image, and it is equivalent to executing `kustomize edit set image`.

The general command syntax is:
```
kustomize edit set image <imageName>=<newRegistry>/<newImageName>:<newTag>
```

For example:
```
kustomize edit set image app-image=my-registry.com/my-app:v1.2.3
```

**Note:** There's a known limitation where the `kustomize edit set image` command does not retain existing `newTag` information when setting only the `newName` value.

content.text:
### Update Busybox Image to Alpine in Kustomization

Source: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/blob/master/examples/image.md

This command modifies the kustomization.yaml file to change the image used by 'busybox' to 'alpine:3.6'. Kustomize will apply this change to all resources referencing the original image name.

```shell
cd $DEMO_HOME
kustomize edit set image busybox=alpine:3.6
```

--------------------------------

### Kustomize CLI: Set Image Tag from Environment Variable (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/blob/master/site/content/en/docs/Reference/API/Kustomization File/images.md

This bash command demonstrates setting an image tag using an environment variable with the Kustomize CLI. This allows dynamic tag management, often used in automated build and deployment processes. The updated configuration is then applied using `kubectl apply -f .`.

```bash
# kustomize edit set image foo:$FOO_IMAGE_TAG
kubectl apply -f .

```

--------------------------------

### Kustomize CLI: Set Image Tag from Git SHA (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/blob/master/site/content/en/docs/Reference/API/Kustomization File/images.md

This bash command uses the Kustomize CLI to set an image tag to the latest git commit SHA. It's a common pattern in CI/CD pipelines to version images by their build commit. The `kubectl apply -f .` command is typically used afterward to apply the updated Kustomization.

```bash
# kustomize edit set image foo:$(git log -n 1 --pretty=format:"%H")
kubectl apply -f .

```

--------------------------------

### Kustomize: Set New Image Tag (YAML)

Source: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/blob/master/site/content/en/docs/Reference/API/Kustomization File/images.md

This YAML snippet illustrates how to update the tag for a container image with Kustomize. It defines the image name and the new tag to be applied, overriding the existing tag.

```yaml
# kustomization.yaml
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization
images:
  - name: mycontainerregistry/myimage
    newTag: v1

```

--------------------------------

### Transform Container Image Names and Tags with Kustomize

Source: https://context7.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/llms.txt

The `images` field in kustomization.yaml allows for transforming container image names, tags, or digests across all resources. This is particularly useful for promoting images through different environments (e.g., development, staging, production) without modifying individual Kubernetes manifests. It supports changing the image name, tag, or digest.

```yaml
# kustomization.yaml
images:
- name: nginx
  newName: my-registry.example.com/nginx
  newTag: 1.21.0
- name: myapp
  newName: myapp-prod
  digest: sha256:abc123def456...
- name: postgres
  newTag: 14-alpine

resources:
- deployment.yaml

# Transforms all matching images:
# Original: image: nginx:latest
# Result: image: my-registry.example.com/nginx:1.21.0

```

input.content:
# Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure - Research

**Researched:** 2026-03-02
**Domain:** GitHub Actions CI/CD, Kustomize image tag automation, ArgoCD GitOps sync
**Confidence:** HIGH

## Summary

Phase 27 closes the GitOps loop: a push to `master` triggers the existing `container-images.yml` workflow which builds and pushes SHA-tagged images to ghcr.io, then a new job in that same workflow updates the Kustomize dev overlay `kustomization.yaml` files with the new SHA-based image tags and commits the change back to the repo. ArgoCD (installed in Phase 26 with automated sync + self-heal) detects the Git change and rolls out updated pods automatically.

The existing infrastructure is well-positioned for this. The `container-images.yml` workflow already builds all three images (apiservice, gateway, web) with `type=sha,prefix=sha-` tags via `docker/metadata-action@v5`, producing tags like `sha-860c190`. The Kustomize dev overlays (`infra/k8s/overlays/dev/{service}/kustomization.yaml`) already have per-service `images:` blocks with `newName` and `newTag` fields. ArgoCD Applications already point to these overlay paths with automated sync enabled. The only missing piece is a GitHub Actions job that updates `newTag` in each overlay's `kustomization.yaml` and commits the change.

A critical design consideration is infinite loop prevention. When the workflow commits updated image tags back to `master`, it must not re-trigger itself. GitHub Actions has built-in protection: events triggered by `GITHUB_TOKEN` do **not** create new workflow runs (except for `workflow_dispatch` and `repository_dispatch`). Since the `container-images.yml` workflow triggers on `push: branches: [master]`, a commit pushed via `GITHUB_TOKEN` will not re-trigger it. As an additional safety measure, the commit message should include `[skip ci]`.

**Primary recommendation:** Add an `update-manifests` job to the existing `container-images.yml` workflow that runs after all three build jobs complete. This job checks out the repo, runs `kustomize edit set image` in each per-service overlay directory to update `newTag` to `sha-<short-sha>`, commits the three modified `kustomization.yaml` files, and pushes to `master` using `GITHUB_TOKEN` with `contents: write` permission. The overlay `newName` fields must also change from local names (`apiservice`, `gateway`, `web`) to the full ghcr.io registry paths.

<phase_requirements>
## Phase Requirements

| ID | Description | Research Support |
|----|-------------|-----------------|
| CICD-02 | CI commits updated SHA image tags to Kustomize dev overlay, triggering ArgoCD sync | The `update-manifests` job uses `kustomize edit set image` to write `sha-<short-sha>` tags into each per-service overlay kustomization.yaml, commits with `[skip ci]`, and pushes via GITHUB_TOKEN. ArgoCD auto-sync detects the tag change within its 3-minute polling interval (or immediately with a webhook) and rolls out new pods. Every image tag in Git matches the commit SHA that built it, providing full traceability. |
</phase_requirements>

## Standard Stack

### Core
| Tool | Version | Purpose | Why Standard |
|------|---------|---------|--------------|
| GitHub Actions | N/A (platform) | CI/CD pipeline; build images + commit manifest updates | Already in use for `container-images.yml`; no new tooling needed |
| Kustomize | built-in (kubectl) | `kustomize edit set image` updates overlay `kustomization.yaml` | Already used in overlays; CLI command is the standard automation pattern for image tag updates |
| docker/metadata-action | v5 | Generates SHA-prefixed image tags (`sha-860c190`) | Already in use in all three build jobs; provides consistent tag format |
| ArgoCD | v3.3.2 | Detects Git changes, syncs cluster state automatically | Installed in Phase 26; auto-sync + self-heal already enabled on all Applications |

### Supporting
| Tool | Version | Purpose | When to Use |
|------|---------|---------|-------------|
| actions/checkout@v4 | v4 | Checkout repo in the update-manifests job | Required to access and modify kustomization.yaml files |
| git (CLI) | built-in | Commit and push updated kustomization.yaml files | Standard approach; no third-party action needed for simple commit+push |

### Alternatives Considered
| Instead of | Could Use | Tradeoff |
|------------|-----------|----------|
| `kustomize edit set image` CLI | Direct sed/yq replacement of newTag | `kustomize edit set image` is the official Kustomize CLI command; sed is fragile and breaks on format changes |
| Commit in same workflow | Separate `repository_dispatch` event | Adds complexity; same-workflow job is simpler and runs only when builds succeed |
| `stefanzweifel/git-auto-commit-action` | Manual `git commit && git push` | Third-party action adds a dependency; manual git commands are ~5 lines and fully transparent |
| ArgoCD polling (3 min default) | GitHub webhook to ArgoCD `/api/webhook` | Webhook gives <5s detection but requires ArgoCD to be internet-accessible; kind cluster is local-only. Polling is sufficient for the success criterion (5 min). Can add webhook later for cloud deployments. |
| Short SHA tag (`sha-860c190`) | Full commit SHA tag | Short SHA (7 chars) is the `docker/metadata-action` default with `type=sha,prefix=sha-`; sufficient for uniqueness and traceability. Already used in the existing workflow. |

**Installation:**
```bash
# No new tools to install. Kustomize is built into the GitHub Actions runner via kubectl.
# If standalone kustomize is needed:
# - uses: imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2
# But kubectl built-in kustomize is sufficient.
```

## Architecture Patterns

### Recommended Workflow Structure
```
.github/workflows/container-images.yml
  build-web:         (existing) Build + push web image to ghcr.io
  build-apiservice:  (existing) Build + push apiservice image to ghcr.io
  build-gateway:     (existing) Build + push gateway image to ghcr.io
  update-manifests:  (NEW) Runs after all 3 builds complete
    - Checkout repo
    - kustomize edit set image for each overlay
    - git commit + push [skip ci]
```

### Pattern 1: Update-Manifests Job with `needs` Dependency
**What:** A GitHub Actions job that depends on all three build jobs (`needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]`), updates Kustomize overlays with the new SHA tag, and commits the change.
**When to use:** When all images in a monorepo are built and deployed together.
**Example:**
```yaml
# Source: Standard GitOps pattern verified via official Kustomize docs + GitHub Actions docs
update-manifests:
  name: Update Kustomize Manifests
  needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  permissions:
    contents: write
  steps:
    - name: Checkout
      uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Update image tags in dev overlay
      env:
        SHORT_SHA: sha-${{ github.sha }}  # Will be trimmed to 7 chars below
      run: |
        SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"

        cd infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice
        kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:$SHORT_SHA

        cd ../gateway
        kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/gateway:$SHORT_SHA

        cd ../web
        kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/web:$SHORT_SHA

    - name: Commit and push
      run: |
        git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
        git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
        git add infra/k8s/overlays/dev/
        git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes to commit" && exit 0
        git commit -m "[skip ci] chore: update image tags to sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
        git push
```

### Pattern 2: Overlay Image Name Correction
**What:** The current dev overlay kustomization.yaml files use local-only image names (`apiservice:dev`, `gateway:dev`, `web:dev`) that match the bootstrap script's local builds. For CI/CD, the `newName` must point to the ghcr.io registry path.
**When to use:** When the same overlay serves both local development (kind with local images) and CI/CD (ghcr.io images).
**Key insight:** `kustomize edit set image` with the syntax `<oldName>=<newName>:<newTag>` updates both `newName` and `newTag` in one command. Running `kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190` updates the overlay from:
```yaml
images:
- name: apiservice
  newName: apiservice
  newTag: dev
```
to:
```yaml
images:
- name: apiservice
  newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice
  newTag: sha-860c190
```
This means the base deployment's `image: apiservice:dev` gets transformed by Kustomize to `image: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190`.

### Pattern 3: SHA Tag Format Alignment
**What:** The `docker/metadata-action` `type=sha,prefix=sha-` generates tags like `sha-860c190` (7-character short SHA). The `update-manifests` job must produce the same format.
**When to use:** Always -- the tag in `kustomization.yaml` must exactly match a tag that exists in ghcr.io.
**Example:**
```bash
# docker/metadata-action produces: sha-860c190 (7 chars)
# To match in shell:
SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
# Result: sha-860c190
```

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
- **Using `latest` tag in kustomization.yaml:** Defeats traceability. Every deployment must map to a specific commit SHA.
- **Committing with a PAT instead of GITHUB_TOKEN:** PAT-based commits trigger subsequent workflows, risking infinite loops. GITHUB_TOKEN commits do not trigger push-based workflows.
- **Running `kustomize edit set image` in the base directory:** The base deployment.yaml has the original image reference; the overlay is where environment-specific image overrides belong.
- **Hardcoding the SHA in the workflow:** Use `${{ github.sha }}` and `cut -c1-7` to derive the short SHA dynamically.
- **Skipping the `git diff --staged --quiet` check:** If no files changed (e.g., re-run of the same commit), the commit step should be a no-op rather than failing.
- **Modifying overlay for tag-only push (v\*.\*.\*):** The workflow triggers on both `push: branches: [master]` and `push: tags: ['v*.*.*']`. The manifest update should only happen for branch pushes to `master`, not for tag pushes (which are release artifacts, not deployments).

## Don't Hand-Roll

| Problem | Don't Build | Use Instead | Why |
|---------|-------------|-------------|-----|
| Image tag update in YAML | Custom sed/awk to replace `newTag:` | `kustomize edit set image` | Handles YAML formatting, multi-line values, comments; official CLI command |
| Git commit automation | Custom shell script with error handling | `git config && git add && git commit && git push` in workflow step | Simple enough for 5 lines; no third-party action needed |
| SHA tag generation | Custom hashing or tag derivation | `docker/metadata-action` `type=sha,prefix=sha-` | Already generates correct format; just derive matching format in update job |
| ArgoCD sync trigger | Custom API call to ArgoCD | ArgoCD's built-in Git polling (3 min) | Polling is sufficient for 5-minute SLA; webhook requires internet-accessible ArgoCD |

**Key insight:** The entire GitOps loop closure is ~30 lines of workflow YAML added to an existing file. No new tools, no new repos, no complex infrastructure. The hard work was done in Phases 23-26.

## Common Pitfalls

### Pitfall 1: Infinite Workflow Loop
**What goes wrong:** The `update-manifests` job commits to `master`, which triggers `container-images.yml` again, which builds images and commits again, forever.
**Why it happens:** If using a PAT or misconfigured token, push events can re-trigger the same workflow.
**How to avoid:** Use `GITHUB_TOKEN` (not a PAT) for the git push. GitHub Actions does not trigger `push`-based workflows for commits made by `GITHUB_TOKEN`. As a belt-and-suspenders measure, include `[skip ci]` in the commit message.
**Warning signs:** Multiple identical workflow runs in quick succession; the Actions tab shows a cascade of runs.

### Pitfall 2: SHA Tag Mismatch
**What goes wrong:** The tag in `kustomization.yaml` (e.g., `sha-860c190`) does not match any tag pushed to ghcr.io, causing `ImagePullBackOff` in the cluster.
**Why it happens:** The `docker/metadata-action` uses a 7-character short SHA by default, but the update-manifests job uses a different length or format.
**How to avoid:** Use `echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7` to produce the same 7-character short SHA. Verify by checking `docker/metadata-action` step output in a previous run.
**Warning signs:** Pods in `ImagePullBackOff`; `kubectl describe pod` shows "manifest unknown" or "not found" for the image tag.

### Pitfall 3: Race Condition on Push
**What goes wrong:** The `update-manifests` job checks out the repo at the triggering commit, but another commit lands on `master` before the push, causing a `push rejected (non-fast-forward)` error.
**Why it happens:** In a high-traffic repo, multiple pushes to `master` can overlap.
**How to avoid:** For this project (single developer), this is extremely unlikely. If it becomes an issue, add `git pull --rebase` before `git push`, or use a retry step. The workflow will simply fail and the next push will succeed.
**Warning signs:** `update-manifests` job fails with "non-fast-forward" error.

### Pitfall 4: Tag Push Triggers Manifest Update
**What goes wrong:** A release tag push (`v1.0.0`) triggers the workflow, and the `update-manifests` job updates overlays with the SHA of the tag commit, overwriting the latest `master` SHA tag.
**Why it happens:** The workflow triggers on both `push: branches: [master]` and `push: tags: ['v*.*.*']`.
**How to avoid:** Add an `if` condition to the `update-manifests` job: `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'`. This ensures manifest updates only happen for branch pushes, not tag pushes.
**Warning signs:** Overlay kustomization.yaml being updated on tag pushes with non-master SHAs.

### Pitfall 5: Overlay `newName` Still Points to Local Image
**What goes wrong:** The overlay has `newName: apiservice` (local Docker name) instead of `newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice` (registry path). ArgoCD syncs the manifest, but the pod tries to pull `apiservice:sha-860c190` from Docker Hub instead of ghcr.io.
**Why it happens:** The current overlays were set up for local kind development where images are loaded directly. CI/CD images live in ghcr.io.
**How to avoid:** `kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-xxx` updates both `newName` and `newTag` simultaneously. This is the correct command to use.
**Warning signs:** Pods in `ImagePullBackOff` with "repository does not exist" error.

### Pitfall 6: Bootstrap Script Conflicts with CI/CD Overlays
**What goes wrong:** The bootstrap script builds local images tagged `dev` and the overlays reference `newTag: dev`. After CI/CD updates the overlay to `sha-xxx`, the bootstrap script no longer works because the local kind cluster doesn't have `ghcr.io/...` images.
**Why it happens:** The overlay serves two purposes: local development and CI/CD deployment.
**How to avoid:** Accept that the overlay in Git reflects the last CI/CD deployment. For local development, the bootstrap script can either: (a) run `kustomize edit set image` to reset overlays to local tags after applying, or (b) be updated to also tag local images with the same format. The simplest approach is to accept that the overlay reflects CI/CD state and the bootstrap script's local image loading works independently (ArgoCD is not used in local kind development without the full CI/CD pipeline).
**Warning signs:** Bootstrap script succeeds but ArgoCD shows OutOfSync because overlay tags don't match loaded images.

## Code Examples

Verified patterns from official sources:

### Complete `update-manifests` Job
```yaml
# Source: Standard GitOps pattern from Kustomize + GitHub Actions docs
update-manifests:
  name: Update Kustomize Manifests
  needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]
  if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  permissions:
    contents: write
  steps:
    - name: Checkout
      uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Update image tags in dev overlay
      run: |
        SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
        echo "Updating image tags to: $SHORT_SHA"

        cd infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice
        kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:$SHORT_SHA

        cd ../gateway
        kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/gateway:$SHORT_SHA

        cd ../web
        kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/web:$SHORT_SHA

    - name: Commit and push
      run: |
        git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
        git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
        git add infra/k8s/overlays/dev/
        git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes to commit" && exit 0
        git commit -m "[skip ci] chore: update image tags to sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
        git push
```

### Kustomize Edit Set Image Command
```bash
# Source: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/blob/master/examples/image.md
# Syntax: kustomize edit set image <oldName>=<newRegistry>/<newName>:<newTag>

# Before (in kustomization.yaml):
# images:
# - name: apiservice
#   newName: apiservice
#   newTag: dev

kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190

# After (in kustomization.yaml):
# images:
# - name: apiservice
#   newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice
#   newTag: sha-860c190
```

### SHA Tag Derivation
```bash
# docker/metadata-action with type=sha,prefix=sha- produces: sha-860c190 (7 chars)
# To match in shell:
SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
# For full SHA: ${{ github.sha }} = 860c1904a1ce19322e91ac35af1ab07466440c37
# Short: sha-860c190
```

### Verifying the Loop End-to-End
```bash
# 1. Check the latest image tag in the overlay
cat infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice/kustomization.yaml
# images:
# - name: apiservice
#   newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice
#   newTag: sha-860c190

# 2. Check ArgoCD sees the updated Application
kubectl get applications -n argocd -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.status.sync.status}{"\n"}{end}'

# 3. Check the running pod's image matches
kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.spec.containers[0].image}{"\n"}{end}'
# apiservice-xxx   ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190
```

## State of the Art

| Old Approach | Current Approach | When Changed | Impact |
|--------------|------------------|--------------|--------|
| Separate config repo for manifests | Single repo (app + infra in same repo) | Ongoing pattern | Simpler for single-team projects; `GITHUB_TOKEN` can commit to same repo |
| Custom webhook handlers for ArgoCD | ArgoCD built-in 3-min polling or `/api/webhook` | ArgoCD 1.x+ | No custom infrastructure needed for GitOps sync trigger |
| `sed -i` to replace image tags | `kustomize edit set image` CLI | Kustomize 3.x+ | YAML-aware, handles formatting, official tool |
| PAT-based commits in CI | GITHUB_TOKEN with `contents: write` | GitHub Actions 2021+ | Built-in infinite loop prevention; no token management overhead |

**Deprecated/outdated:**
- Using `sed` to modify Kustomize files: fragile, not YAML-aware
- Separate GitOps repository for manifests: adds complexity for single-team projects; single-repo is standard for small projects
- ArgoCD Image Updater (write-back): designed for auto-updating to latest tags; not needed when CI explicitly sets the tag

## Open Questions

1. **Bootstrap script compatibility after CI/CD overlay update**
   - What we know: The bootstrap script builds local images tagged `dev` and uses overlays with `newTag: dev`. After CI/CD, the overlay will have `newTag: sha-xxx` pointing to ghcr.io.
   - What's unclear: Should the bootstrap script be updated to handle this divergence, or is it acceptable that the overlay in Git reflects CI/CD state?
   - Recommendation: Accept the divergence. The bootstrap script's Step 10-11 (build/load images) and Step 12 (apply ArgoCD root app) work independently. For local development, the user can either: (a) let the bootstrap script work as-is (ArgoCD will sync from Git, pulling ghcr.io images if the cluster has internet), or (b) after bootstrap, manually reset overlays with `kustomize edit set image apiservice=apiservice:dev` etc. This is a local dev workflow concern, not a CI/CD concern. Defer to implementation.

2. **ArgoCD sync latency vs 5-minute SLA**
   - What we know: ArgoCD polls Git every 3 minutes by default. The success criterion requires a new pod within 5 minutes of CI workflow completing.
   - What's unclear: Whether 3-minute polling + pod rollout time fits within 5 minutes.
   - Recommendation: 3-minute polling + ~30s for pod rollout = ~3.5 minutes typical, well within the 5-minute SLA. If tighter latency is needed later, configure a GitHub webhook to ArgoCD's `/api/webhook` endpoint. Not needed for this phase.

3. **`contents: write` permission scope**
   - What we know: The `update-manifests` job needs `permissions: contents: write` to push commits. The build jobs currently have `permissions: contents: read, packages: write`.
   - What's unclear: Whether job-level permissions can differ from workflow-level permissions.
   - Recommendation: GitHub Actions supports per-job permissions. Set `contents: write` only on the `update-manifests` job. The build jobs keep their existing `contents: read` + `packages: write`.

## Validation Architecture

### Test Framework
| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Framework | Manual validation via GitHub Actions run + kubectl (infrastructure/CI phase, no unit test framework) |
| Config file | `.github/workflows/container-images.yml` |
| Quick run command | Check workflow run status in GitHub Actions tab |
| Full suite command | Push to master, verify: (1) workflow completes, (2) overlay kustomization.yaml updated in Git, (3) ArgoCD syncs, (4) pods running new image |

### Phase Requirements to Test Map
| Req ID | Behavior | Test Type | Automated Command | File Exists? |
|--------|----------|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
| CICD-02 | CI commits updated SHA image tags to Kustomize dev overlay | smoke | `git log --oneline -1 -- infra/k8s/overlays/dev/` (verify [skip ci] commit) | N/A -- Git history |
| CICD-02 | Image tag in overlay matches commit SHA | smoke | `grep newTag infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice/kustomization.yaml` (verify sha-XXX format) | N/A -- file content |
| CICD-02 | ArgoCD syncs updated manifests | smoke | `kubectl get applications -n argocd -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.sync.status}'` | N/A -- kubectl |
| CICD-02 | Pod runs image matching commit SHA | smoke | `kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[0].image}'` | N/A -- kubectl |
| CICD-02 | Workflow does not trigger infinite loop | smoke | Check GitHub Actions tab shows no cascading runs after the [skip ci] commit | N/A -- GitHub UI |

### Sampling Rate
- **Per task commit:** Verify workflow YAML syntax with `act --dryrun` or visual review
- **Per wave merge:** Push to master, verify full loop: build -> commit -> ArgoCD sync -> pod update
- **Phase gate:** Both success criteria verified before `/gsd:verify-work`

### Wave 0 Gaps
None -- this phase modifies a GitHub Actions workflow file and Kustomize overlays. No test framework infrastructure needed.

## Sources

### Primary (HIGH confidence)
- [/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/blob/master/examples/image.md) - `kustomize edit set image` command syntax and behavior verified via Context7
- [docker/metadata-action@v5](https://github.com/docker/metadata-action) - SHA tag format: `type=sha,prefix=sha-` produces `sha-<7chars>` by default
- [GitHub Actions: Skipping workflow runs](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/managing-workflow-runs-and-deployments/managing-workflow-runs/skipping-workflow-runs) - `[skip ci]` in commit message skips push/PR-triggered workflows
- [GitHub Actions: GITHUB_TOKEN](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/25702) - Events triggered by GITHUB_TOKEN do not create new workflow runs (except workflow_dispatch/repository_dispatch)
- [ArgoCD Git Webhook Configuration](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/latest/operator-manual/webhook/) - Webhook endpoint at `/api/webhook` for immediate sync (optional, not needed for this phase)
- [ArgoCD Auto Sync](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user-guide/auto_sync/) - Default 3-minute polling interval; selfHeal and automated sync configuration

### Secondary (MEDIUM confidence)
- [GitOps CI/CD patterns with GitHub Actions + ArgoCD](https://medium.com/@mehmetkanus17/argocd-github-actions-a-complete-gitops-ci-cd-workflow-for-kubernetes-applications-ed2f91d37641) - Workflow structure with `needs:` dependency for update-manifest job
- [Automation with GitHub Actions and Kustomize](https://gap.gjensidige.io/docs/guides/ci-automation-with-kustomize) - `kustomize edit set image` in CI/CD pipelines pattern

### Tertiary (LOW confidence)
- None -- all findings verified with official documentation

## Metadata

**Confidence breakdown:**
- Standard stack: HIGH - No new tools needed; existing `container-images.yml`, Kustomize, and ArgoCD are all already in place. Only addition is a new job in an existing workflow.
- Architecture: HIGH - The `update-manifests` job pattern is the standard GitOps CI/CD pattern, verified across multiple official and community sources. `kustomize edit set image` syntax verified via Context7.
- Pitfalls: HIGH - Infinite loop prevention via GITHUB_TOKEN is documented by GitHub. SHA format alignment is straightforward. Race condition is theoretical for single-developer repo.

**Research date:** 2026-03-02
**Valid until:** 2026-04-02 (30 days -- GitHub Actions and Kustomize are stable; ArgoCD sync behavior is well-established)

content.text:
## RESEARCH COMPLETE

**Phase:** 27 - CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure
**Confidence:** HIGH

### Key Findings
- **No new tools needed.** The entire GitOps loop closure is ~30 lines of YAML added as a new `update-manifests` job in the existing `container-images.yml` workflow. All infrastructure (Kustomize overlays, ArgoCD auto-sync, ghcr.io image registry) is already in place from Phases 23-26.
- **`kustomize edit set image` updates both `newName` and `newTag` simultaneously.** Running `kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190` transforms the overlay from local image names (`apiservice:dev`) to full registry paths with SHA tags.
- **Infinite loop prevention is built-in.** GitHub Actions does not trigger push-based workflows for commits made via `GITHUB_TOKEN`. The `[skip ci]` commit message provides belt-and-suspenders safety.
- **SHA tag format alignment is critical.** The `docker/metadata-action` `type=sha,prefix=sha-` produces 7-character short SHA tags (e.g., `sha-860c190`). The update job must derive the same format with `cut -c1-7`.
- **ArgoCD's 3-minute polling interval fits the 5-minute SLA.** 3 min polling + ~30s pod rollout = ~3.5 min typical, well within the success criterion.

### File Created
`.planning/phases/27-cicd-gitops-loop-closure/27-RESEARCH.md`

### Confidence Assessment
| Area | Level | Reason |
|------|-------|--------|
| Standard Stack | HIGH | No new tools; existing workflow, Kustomize, ArgoCD all verified |
| Architecture | HIGH | `update-manifests` job pattern verified via Context7, GitHub docs, community patterns |
| Pitfalls | HIGH | Infinite loop prevention via GITHUB_TOKEN is GitHub-documented behavior; SHA format verified |

### Open Questions
1. Bootstrap script compatibility after CI/CD updates overlay tags from `dev` to `sha-xxx` (recommend: accept divergence, local dev is separate concern)
2. ArgoCD polling latency vs 5-minute SLA (recommend: 3-min polling is sufficient; webhook is optional enhancement)
3. Per-job `contents: write` permission scope (recommend: set per-job, not workflow-level)

### Ready for Planning
Research complete. Planner can now create PLAN.md files.

1→# Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure - Research
     2→
     3→**Researched:** 2026-03-02
     4→**Domain:** GitHub Actions CI/CD, Kustomize image tag automation, ArgoCD GitOps sync
     5→**Confidence:** HIGH
     6→
     7→## Summary
     8→
     9→Phase 27 closes the GitOps loop: a push to `master` triggers the existing `container-images.yml` workflow which builds and pushes SHA-tagged images to ghcr.io, then a new job in that same workflow updates the Kustomize dev overlay `kustomization.yaml` files with the new SHA-based image tags and commits the change back to the repo. ArgoCD (installed in Phase 26 with automated sync + self-heal) detects the Git change and rolls out updated pods automatically.
    10→
    11→The existing infrastructure is well-positioned for this. The `container-images.yml` workflow already builds all three images (apiservice, gateway, web) with `type=sha,prefix=sha-` tags via `docker/metadata-action@v5`, producing tags like `sha-860c190`. The Kustomize dev overlays (`infra/k8s/overlays/dev/{service}/kustomization.yaml`) already have per-service `images:` blocks with `newName` and `newTag` fields. ArgoCD Applications already point to these overlay paths with automated sync enabled. The only missing piece is a GitHub Actions job that updates `newTag` in each overlay's `kustomization.yaml` and commits the change.
    12→
    13→A critical design consideration is infinite loop prevention. When the workflow commits updated image tags back to `master`, it must not re-trigger itself. GitHub Actions has built-in protection: events triggered by `GITHUB_TOKEN` do **not** create new workflow runs (except for `workflow_dispatch` and `repository_dispatch`). Since the `container-images.yml` workflow triggers on `push: branches: [master]`, a commit pushed via `GITHUB_TOKEN` will not re-trigger it. As an additional safety measure, the commit message should include `[skip ci]`.
    14→
    15→**Primary recommendation:** Add an `update-manifests` job to the existing `container-images.yml` workflow that runs after all three build jobs complete. This job checks out the repo, runs `kustomize edit set image` in each per-service overlay directory to update `newTag` to `sha-<short-sha>`, commits the three modified `kustomization.yaml` files, and pushes to `master` using `GITHUB_TOKEN` with `contents: write` permission. The overlay `newName` fields must also change from local names (`apiservice`, `gateway`, `web`) to the full ghcr.io registry paths.
    16→
    17→<phase_requirements>
    18→## Phase Requirements
    19→
    20→| ID | Description | Research Support |
    21→|----|-------------|-----------------|
    22→| CICD-02 | CI commits updated SHA image tags to Kustomize dev overlay, triggering ArgoCD sync | The `update-manifests` job uses `kustomize edit set image` to write `sha-<short-sha>` tags into each per-service overlay kustomization.yaml, commits with `[skip ci]`, and pushes via GITHUB_TOKEN. ArgoCD auto-sync detects the tag change within its 3-minute polling interval (or immediately with a webhook) and rolls out new pods. Every image tag in Git matches the commit SHA that built it, providing full traceability. |
    23→</phase_requirements>
    24→
    25→## Standard Stack
    26→
    27→### Core
    28→| Tool | Version | Purpose | Why Standard |
    29→|------|---------|---------|--------------|
    30→| GitHub Actions | N/A (platform) | CI/CD pipeline; build images + commit manifest updates | Already in use for `container-images.yml`; no new tooling needed |
    31→| Kustomize | built-in (kubectl) | `kustomize edit set image` updates overlay `kustomization.yaml` | Already used in overlays; CLI command is the standard automation pattern for image tag updates |
    32→| docker/metadata-action | v5 | Generates SHA-prefixed image tags (`sha-860c190`) | Already in use in all three build jobs; provides consistent tag format |
    33→| ArgoCD | v3.3.2 | Detects Git changes, syncs cluster state automatically | Installed in Phase 26; auto-sync + self-heal already enabled on all Applications |
    34→
    35→### Supporting
    36→| Tool | Version | Purpose | When to Use |
    37→|------|---------|---------|-------------|
    38→| actions/checkout@v4 | v4 | Checkout repo in the update-manifests job | Required to access and modify kustomization.yaml files |
    39→| git (CLI) | built-in | Commit and push updated kustomization.yaml files | Standard approach; no third-party action needed for simple commit+push |
    40→
    41→### Alternatives Considered
    42→| Instead of | Could Use | Tradeoff |
    43→|------------|-----------|----------|
    44→| `kustomize edit set image` CLI | Direct sed/yq replacement of newTag | `kustomize edit set image` is the official Kustomize CLI command; sed is fragile and breaks on format changes |
    45→| Commit in same workflow | Separate `repository_dispatch` event | Adds complexity; same-workflow job is simpler and runs only when builds succeed |
    46→| `stefanzweifel/git-auto-commit-action` | Manual `git commit && git push` | Third-party action adds a dependency; manual git commands are ~5 lines and fully transparent |
    47→| ArgoCD polling (3 min default) | GitHub webhook to ArgoCD `/api/webhook` | Webhook gives <5s detection but requires ArgoCD to be internet-accessible; kind cluster is local-only. Polling is sufficient for the success criterion (5 min). Can add webhook later for cloud deployments. |
    48→| Short SHA tag (`sha-860c190`) | Full commit SHA tag | Short SHA (7 chars) is the `docker/metadata-action` default with `type=sha,prefix=sha-`; sufficient for uniqueness and traceability. Already used in the existing workflow. |
    49→
    50→**Installation:**
    51→```bash
    52→# No new tools to install. Kustomize is built into the GitHub Actions runner via kubectl.
    53→# If standalone kustomize is needed:
    54→# - uses: imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2
    55→# But kubectl built-in kustomize is sufficient.
    56→```
    57→
    58→## Architecture Patterns
    59→
    60→### Recommended Workflow Structure
    61→```
    62→.github/workflows/container-images.yml
    63→  build-web:         (existing) Build + push web image to ghcr.io
    64→  build-apiservice:  (existing) Build + push apiservice image to ghcr.io
    65→  build-gateway:     (existing) Build + push gateway image to ghcr.io
    66→  update-manifests:  (NEW) Runs after all 3 builds complete
    67→    - Checkout repo
    68→    - kustomize edit set image for each overlay
    69→    - git commit + push [skip ci]
    70→```
    71→
    72→### Pattern 1: Update-Manifests Job with `needs` Dependency
    73→**What:** A GitHub Actions job that depends on all three build jobs (`needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]`), updates Kustomize overlays with the new SHA tag, and commits the change.
    74→**When to use:** When all images in a monorepo are built and deployed together.
    75→**Example:**
    76→```yaml
    77→# Source: Standard GitOps pattern verified via official Kustomize docs + GitHub Actions docs
    78→update-manifests:
    79→  name: Update Kustomize Manifests
    80→  needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]
    81→  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    82→  permissions:
    83→    contents: write
    84→  steps:
    85→    - name: Checkout
    86→      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    87→
    88→    - name: Update image tags in dev overlay
    89→      env:
    90→        SHORT_SHA: sha-${{ github.sha }}  # Will be trimmed to 7 chars below
    91→      run: |
    92→        SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
    93→
    94→        cd infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice
    95→        kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:$SHORT_SHA
    96→
    97→        cd ../gateway
    98→        kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/gateway:$SHORT_SHA
    99→
   100→        cd ../web
   101→        kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/web:$SHORT_SHA
   102→
   103→    - name: Commit and push
   104→      run: |
   105→        git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
   106→        git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
   107→        git add infra/k8s/overlays/dev/
   108→        git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes to commit" && exit 0
   109→        git commit -m "[skip ci] chore: update image tags to sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   110→        git push
   111→```
   112→
   113→### Pattern 2: Overlay Image Name Correction
   114→**What:** The current dev overlay kustomization.yaml files use local-only image names (`apiservice:dev`, `gateway:dev`, `web:dev`) that match the bootstrap script's local builds. For CI/CD, the `newName` must point to the ghcr.io registry path.
   115→**When to use:** When the same overlay serves both local development (kind with local images) and CI/CD (ghcr.io images).
   116→**Key insight:** `kustomize edit set image` with the syntax `<oldName>=<newName>:<newTag>` updates both `newName` and `newTag` in one command. Running `kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190` updates the overlay from:
   117→```yaml
   118→images:
   119→- name: apiservice
   120→  newName: apiservice
   121→  newTag: dev
   122→```
   123→to:
   124→```yaml
   125→images:
   126→- name: apiservice
   127→  newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice
   128→  newTag: sha-860c190
   129→```
   130→This means the base deployment's `image: apiservice:dev` gets transformed by Kustomize to `image: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190`.
   131→
   132→### Pattern 3: SHA Tag Format Alignment
   133→**What:** The `docker/metadata-action` `type=sha,prefix=sha-` generates tags like `sha-860c190` (7-character short SHA). The `update-manifests` job must produce the same format.
   134→**When to use:** Always -- the tag in `kustomization.yaml` must exactly match a tag that exists in ghcr.io.
   135→**Example:**
   136→```bash
   137→# docker/metadata-action produces: sha-860c190 (7 chars)
   138→# To match in shell:
   139→SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   140→# Result: sha-860c190
   141→```
   142→
   143→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   144→- **Using `latest` tag in kustomization.yaml:** Defeats traceability. Every deployment must map to a specific commit SHA.
   145→- **Committing with a PAT instead of GITHUB_TOKEN:** PAT-based commits trigger subsequent workflows, risking infinite loops. GITHUB_TOKEN commits do not trigger push-based workflows.
   146→- **Running `kustomize edit set image` in the base directory:** The base deployment.yaml has the original image reference; the overlay is where environment-specific image overrides belong.
   147→- **Hardcoding the SHA in the workflow:** Use `${{ github.sha }}` and `cut -c1-7` to derive the short SHA dynamically.
   148→- **Skipping the `git diff --staged --quiet` check:** If no files changed (e.g., re-run of the same commit), the commit step should be a no-op rather than failing.
   149→- **Modifying overlay for tag-only push (v\*.\*.\*):** The workflow triggers on both `push: branches: [master]` and `push: tags: ['v*.*.*']`. The manifest update should only happen for branch pushes to `master`, not for tag pushes (which are release artifacts, not deployments).
   150→
   151→## Don't Hand-Roll
   152→
   153→| Problem | Don't Build | Use Instead | Why |
   154→|---------|-------------|-------------|-----|
   155→| Image tag update in YAML | Custom sed/awk to replace `newTag:` | `kustomize edit set image` | Handles YAML formatting, multi-line values, comments; official CLI command |
   156→| Git commit automation | Custom shell script with error handling | `git config && git add && git commit && git push` in workflow step | Simple enough for 5 lines; no third-party action needed |
   157→| SHA tag generation | Custom hashing or tag derivation | `docker/metadata-action` `type=sha,prefix=sha-` | Already generates correct format; just derive matching format in update job |
   158→| ArgoCD sync trigger | Custom API call to ArgoCD | ArgoCD's built-in Git polling (3 min) | Polling is sufficient for 5-minute SLA; webhook requires internet-accessible ArgoCD |
   159→
   160→**Key insight:** The entire GitOps loop closure is ~30 lines of workflow YAML added to an existing file. No new tools, no new repos, no complex infrastructure. The hard work was done in Phases 23-26.
   161→
   162→## Common Pitfalls
   163→
   164→### Pitfall 1: Infinite Workflow Loop
   165→**What goes wrong:** The `update-manifests` job commits to `master`, which triggers `container-images.yml` again, which builds images and commits again, forever.
   166→**Why it happens:** If using a PAT or misconfigured token, push events can re-trigger the same workflow.
   167→**How to avoid:** Use `GITHUB_TOKEN` (not a PAT) for the git push. GitHub Actions does not trigger `push`-based workflows for commits made by `GITHUB_TOKEN`. As a belt-and-suspenders measure, include `[skip ci]` in the commit message.
   168→**Warning signs:** Multiple identical workflow runs in quick succession; the Actions tab shows a cascade of runs.
   169→
   170→### Pitfall 2: SHA Tag Mismatch
   171→**What goes wrong:** The tag in `kustomization.yaml` (e.g., `sha-860c190`) does not match any tag pushed to ghcr.io, causing `ImagePullBackOff` in the cluster.
   172→**Why it happens:** The `docker/metadata-action` uses a 7-character short SHA by default, but the update-manifests job uses a different length or format.
   173→**How to avoid:** Use `echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7` to produce the same 7-character short SHA. Verify by checking `docker/metadata-action` step output in a previous run.
   174→**Warning signs:** Pods in `ImagePullBackOff`; `kubectl describe pod` shows "manifest unknown" or "not found" for the image tag.
   175→
   176→### Pitfall 3: Race Condition on Push
   177→**What goes wrong:** The `update-manifests` job checks out the repo at the triggering commit, but another commit lands on `master` before the push, causing a `push rejected (non-fast-forward)` error.
   178→**Why it happens:** In a high-traffic repo, multiple pushes to `master` can overlap.
   179→**How to avoid:** For this project (single developer), this is extremely unlikely. If it becomes an issue, add `git pull --rebase` before `git push`, or use a retry step. The workflow will simply fail and the next push will succeed.
   180→**Warning signs:** `update-manifests` job fails with "non-fast-forward" error.
   181→
   182→### Pitfall 4: Tag Push Triggers Manifest Update
   183→**What goes wrong:** A release tag push (`v1.0.0`) triggers the workflow, and the `update-manifests` job updates overlays with the SHA of the tag commit, overwriting the latest `master` SHA tag.
   184→**Why it happens:** The workflow triggers on both `push: branches: [master]` and `push: tags: ['v*.*.*']`.
   185→**How to avoid:** Add an `if` condition to the `update-manifests` job: `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'`. This ensures manifest updates only happen for branch pushes, not tag pushes.
   186→**Warning signs:** Overlay kustomization.yaml being updated on tag pushes with non-master SHAs.
   187→
   188→### Pitfall 5: Overlay `newName` Still Points to Local Image
   189→**What goes wrong:** The overlay has `newName: apiservice` (local Docker name) instead of `newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice` (registry path). ArgoCD syncs the manifest, but the pod tries to pull `apiservice:sha-860c190` from Docker Hub instead of ghcr.io.
   190→**Why it happens:** The current overlays were set up for local kind development where images are loaded directly. CI/CD images live in ghcr.io.
   191→**How to avoid:** `kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-xxx` updates both `newName` and `newTag` simultaneously. This is the correct command to use.
   192→**Warning signs:** Pods in `ImagePullBackOff` with "repository does not exist" error.
   193→
   194→### Pitfall 6: Bootstrap Script Conflicts with CI/CD Overlays
   195→**What goes wrong:** The bootstrap script builds local images tagged `dev` and the overlays reference `newTag: dev`. After CI/CD updates the overlay to `sha-xxx`, the bootstrap script no longer works because the local kind cluster doesn't have `ghcr.io/...` images.
   196→**Why it happens:** The overlay serves two purposes: local development and CI/CD deployment.
   197→**How to avoid:** Accept that the overlay in Git reflects the last CI/CD deployment. For local development, the bootstrap script can either: (a) run `kustomize edit set image` to reset overlays to local tags after applying, or (b) be updated to also tag local images with the same format. The simplest approach is to accept that the overlay reflects CI/CD state and the bootstrap script's local image loading works independently (ArgoCD is not used in local kind development without the full CI/CD pipeline).
   198→**Warning signs:** Bootstrap script succeeds but ArgoCD shows OutOfSync because overlay tags don't match loaded images.
   199→
   200→## Code Examples
   201→
   202→Verified patterns from official sources:
   203→
   204→### Complete `update-manifests` Job
   205→```yaml
   206→# Source: Standard GitOps pattern from Kustomize + GitHub Actions docs
   207→update-manifests:
   208→  name: Update Kustomize Manifests
   209→  needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]
   210→  if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'
   211→  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   212→  permissions:
   213→    contents: write
   214→  steps:
   215→    - name: Checkout
   216→      uses: actions/checkout@v4
   217→
   218→    - name: Update image tags in dev overlay
   219→      run: |
   220→        SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   221→        echo "Updating image tags to: $SHORT_SHA"
   222→
   223→        cd infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice
   224→        kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:$SHORT_SHA
   225→
   226→        cd ../gateway
   227→        kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/gateway:$SHORT_SHA
   228→
   229→        cd ../web
   230→        kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/web:$SHORT_SHA
   231→
   232→    - name: Commit and push
   233→      run: |
   234→        git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
   235→        git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
   236→        git add infra/k8s/overlays/dev/
   237→        git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes to commit" && exit 0
   238→        git commit -m "[skip ci] chore: update image tags to sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   239→        git push
   240→```
   241→
   242→### Kustomize Edit Set Image Command
   243→```bash
   244→# Source: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/blob/master/examples/image.md
   245→# Syntax: kustomize edit set image <oldName>=<newRegistry>/<newName>:<newTag>
   246→
   247→# Before (in kustomization.yaml):
   248→# images:
   249→# - name: apiservice
   250→#   newName: apiservice
   251→#   newTag: dev
   252→
   253→kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190
   254→
   255→# After (in kustomization.yaml):
   256→# images:
   257→# - name: apiservice
   258→#   newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice
   259→#   newTag: sha-860c190
   260→```
   261→
   262→### SHA Tag Derivation
   263→```bash
   264→# docker/metadata-action with type=sha,prefix=sha- produces: sha-860c190 (7 chars)
   265→# To match in shell:
   266→SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   267→# For full SHA: ${{ github.sha }} = 860c1904a1ce19322e91ac35af1ab07466440c37
   268→# Short: sha-860c190
   269→```
   270→
   271→### Verifying the Loop End-to-End
   272→```bash
   273→# 1. Check the latest image tag in the overlay
   274→cat infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice/kustomization.yaml
   275→# images:
   276→# - name: apiservice
   277→#   newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice
   278→#   newTag: sha-860c190
   279→
   280→# 2. Check ArgoCD sees the updated Application
   281→kubectl get applications -n argocd -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.status.sync.status}{"\n"}{end}'
   282→
   283→# 3. Check the running pod's image matches
   284→kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.spec.containers[0].image}{"\n"}{end}'
   285→# apiservice-xxx   ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190
   286→```
   287→
   288→## State of the Art
   289→
   290→| Old Approach | Current Approach | When Changed | Impact |
   291→|--------------|------------------|--------------|--------|
   292→| Separate config repo for manifests | Single repo (app + infra in same repo) | Ongoing pattern | Simpler for single-team projects; `GITHUB_TOKEN` can commit to same repo |
   293→| Custom webhook handlers for ArgoCD | ArgoCD built-in 3-min polling or `/api/webhook` | ArgoCD 1.x+ | No custom infrastructure needed for GitOps sync trigger |
   294→| `sed -i` to replace image tags | `kustomize edit set image` CLI | Kustomize 3.x+ | YAML-aware, handles formatting, official tool |
   295→| PAT-based commits in CI | GITHUB_TOKEN with `contents: write` | GitHub Actions 2021+ | Built-in infinite loop prevention; no token management overhead |
   296→
   297→**Deprecated/outdated:**
   298→- Using `sed` to modify Kustomize files: fragile, not YAML-aware
   299→- Separate GitOps repository for manifests: adds complexity for single-team projects; single-repo is standard for small projects
   300→- ArgoCD Image Updater (write-back): designed for auto-updating to latest tags; not needed when CI explicitly sets the tag
   301→
   302→## Open Questions
   303→
   304→1. **Bootstrap script compatibility after CI/CD overlay update**
   305→   - What we know: The bootstrap script builds local images tagged `dev` and uses overlays with `newTag: dev`. After CI/CD, the overlay will have `newTag: sha-xxx` pointing to ghcr.io.
   306→   - What's unclear: Should the bootstrap script be updated to handle this divergence, or is it acceptable that the overlay in Git reflects CI/CD state?
   307→   - Recommendation: Accept the divergence. The bootstrap script's Step 10-11 (build/load images) and Step 12 (apply ArgoCD root app) work independently. For local development, the user can either: (a) let the bootstrap script work as-is (ArgoCD will sync from Git, pulling ghcr.io images if the cluster has internet), or (b) after bootstrap, manually reset overlays with `kustomize edit set image apiservice=apiservice:dev` etc. This is a local dev workflow concern, not a CI/CD concern. Defer to implementation.
   308→
   309→2. **ArgoCD sync latency vs 5-minute SLA**
   310→   - What we know: ArgoCD polls Git every 3 minutes by default. The success criterion requires a new pod within 5 minutes of CI workflow completing.
   311→   - What's unclear: Whether 3-minute polling + pod rollout time fits within 5 minutes.
   312→   - Recommendation: 3-minute polling + ~30s for pod rollout = ~3.5 minutes typical, well within the 5-minute SLA. If tighter latency is needed later, configure a GitHub webhook to ArgoCD's `/api/webhook` endpoint. Not needed for this phase.
   313→
   314→3. **`contents: write` permission scope**
   315→   - What we know: The `update-manifests` job needs `permissions: contents: write` to push commits. The build jobs currently have `permissions: contents: read, packages: write`.
   316→   - What's unclear: Whether job-level permissions can differ from workflow-level permissions.
   317→   - Recommendation: GitHub Actions supports per-job permissions. Set `contents: write` only on the `update-manifests` job. The build jobs keep their existing `contents: read` + `packages: write`.
   318→
   319→## Validation Architecture
   320→
   321→### Test Framework
   322→| Property | Value |
   323→|----------|-------|
   324→| Framework | Manual validation via GitHub Actions run + kubectl (infrastructure/CI phase, no unit test framework) |
   325→| Config file | `.github/workflows/container-images.yml` |
   326→| Quick run command | Check workflow run status in GitHub Actions tab |
   327→| Full suite command | Push to master, verify: (1) workflow completes, (2) overlay kustomization.yaml updated in Git, (3) ArgoCD syncs, (4) pods running new image |
   328→
   329→### Phase Requirements to Test Map
   330→| Req ID | Behavior | Test Type | Automated Command | File Exists? |
   331→|--------|----------|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
   332→| CICD-02 | CI commits updated SHA image tags to Kustomize dev overlay | smoke | `git log --oneline -1 -- infra/k8s/overlays/dev/` (verify [skip ci] commit) | N/A -- Git history |
   333→| CICD-02 | Image tag in overlay matches commit SHA | smoke | `grep newTag infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice/kustomization.yaml` (verify sha-XXX format) | N/A -- file content |
   334→| CICD-02 | ArgoCD syncs updated manifests | smoke | `kubectl get applications -n argocd -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.sync.status}'` | N/A -- kubectl |
   335→| CICD-02 | Pod runs image matching commit SHA | smoke | `kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[0].image}'` | N/A -- kubectl |
   336→| CICD-02 | Workflow does not trigger infinite loop | smoke | Check GitHub Actions tab shows no cascading runs after the [skip ci] commit | N/A -- GitHub UI |
   337→
   338→### Sampling Rate
   339→- **Per task commit:** Verify workflow YAML syntax with `act --dryrun` or visual review
   340→- **Per wave merge:** Push to master, verify full loop: build -> commit -> ArgoCD sync -> pod update
   341→- **Phase gate:** Both success criteria verified before `/gsd:verify-work`
   342→
   343→### Wave 0 Gaps
   344→None -- this phase modifies a GitHub Actions workflow file and Kustomize overlays. No test framework infrastructure needed.
   345→
   346→## Sources
   347→
   348→### Primary (HIGH confidence)
   349→- [/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/blob/master/examples/image.md) - `kustomize edit set image` command syntax and behavior verified via Context7
   350→- [docker/metadata-action@v5](https://github.com/docker/metadata-action) - SHA tag format: `type=sha,prefix=sha-` produces `sha-<7chars>` by default
   351→- [GitHub Actions: Skipping workflow runs](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/managing-workflow-runs-and-deployments/managing-workflow-runs/skipping-workflow-runs) - `[skip ci]` in commit message skips push/PR-triggered workflows
   352→- [GitHub Actions: GITHUB_TOKEN](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/25702) - Events triggered by GITHUB_TOKEN do not create new workflow runs (except workflow_dispatch/repository_dispatch)
   353→- [ArgoCD Git Webhook Configuration](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/latest/operator-manual/webhook/) - Webhook endpoint at `/api/webhook` for immediate sync (optional, not needed for this phase)
   354→- [ArgoCD Auto Sync](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user-guide/auto_sync/) - Default 3-minute polling interval; selfHeal and automated sync configuration
   355→
   356→### Secondary (MEDIUM confidence)
   357→- [GitOps CI/CD patterns with GitHub Actions + ArgoCD](https://medium.com/@mehmetkanus17/argocd-github-actions-a-complete-gitops-ci-cd-workflow-for-kubernetes-applications-ed2f91d37641) - Workflow structure with `needs:` dependency for update-manifest job
   358→- [Automation with GitHub Actions and Kustomize](https://gap.gjensidige.io/docs/guides/ci-automation-with-kustomize) - `kustomize edit set image` in CI/CD pipelines pattern
   359→
   360→### Tertiary (LOW confidence)
   361→- None -- all findings verified with official documentation
   362→
   363→## Metadata
   364→
   365→**Confidence breakdown:**
   366→- Standard stack: HIGH - No new tools needed; existing `container-images.yml`, Kustomize, and ArgoCD are all already in place. Only addition is a new job in an existing workflow.
   367→- Architecture: HIGH - The `update-manifests` job pattern is the standard GitOps CI/CD pattern, verified across multiple official and community sources. `kustomize edit set image` syntax verified via Context7.
   368→- Pitfalls: HIGH - Infinite loop prevention via GITHUB_TOKEN is documented by GitHub. SHA format alignment is straightforward. Race condition is theoretical for single-developer repo.
   369→
   370→**Research date:** 2026-03-02
   371→**Valid until:** 2026-04-02 (30 days -- GitHub Actions and Kustomize are stable; ArgoCD sync behavior is well-established)

   114→    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/rabbitmq/sealed-secret.yaml"
   115→    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/keycloak/sealed-secret.yaml"
   116→    ```
   117→    WHY: sealed-secret.yaml is no longer referenced in base kustomization.yaml files (removed in Plan 01), so they must be applied explicitly. These must exist in the cluster before ArgoCD syncs the Deployments that reference the Secrets.
   118→
   119→    **Step 7: REMOVE** the old `kubectl apply -k "$SCRIPT_DIR/base/"` line. ArgoCD will handle this now.
   120→
   121→    **Step 8: REMOVE** the old infra pod wait commands. ArgoCD sync handles deployment.

   124→    ```bash
   125→    # --- Step 7: Install ArgoCD ---
   126→    ARGOCD_VERSION="v3.3.2"
   127→    info "Installing ArgoCD ${ARGOCD_VERSION}..."
   128→    kubectl create namespace argocd --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
   129→    kubectl apply -n argocd --server-side --force-conflicts \
   130→      -f "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/argoproj/argo-cd/${ARGOCD_VERSION}/manifests/install.yaml"
   131→    ```
   132→    Use `--server-side --force-conflicts` because ArgoCD CRDs exceed 256KB annotation limit for client-side apply. Use idempotent namespace creation same as Step 5 pattern.
   133→
   134→    **NEW Step 8: Wait for ArgoCD to be ready**
   135→    ```bash
   136→    info "Waiting for ArgoCD to be ready..."
   137→    kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-server -n argocd --timeout=120s
   138→    kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-repo-server -n argocd --timeout=120s
   139→    kubectl rollout status deployment argocd-applicationset-controller -n argocd --timeout=120s

   148→    kubectl apply -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/argocd/argocd-server-nodeport.yaml"

   158→    ```
   159→    This triggers ArgoCD to discover and sync all child Applications, which in turn deploy all infrastructure and application manifests from Git.
   160→
   161→    **NEW Step 13: Wait for ArgoCD to sync all applications**
   162→    ```bash
   163→    info "Waiting for ArgoCD to sync all applications..."

   167→    # Wait for infrastructure pods

   173→

   179→    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=apiservice -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=180s
   180→
   181→    info "Waiting for Gateway..."
   182→    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=gateway -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   183→
   184→    info "Waiting for Web..."
   185→    kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=web -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   186→    ```
   187→    Keep pod wait commands because the user needs to know when everything is ready. ArgoCD sync triggers deployment, but we still wait for pods to be Running.

<objective>
Add an update-manifests job to the existing container-images.yml workflow that commits SHA-based image tags to the Kustomize dev overlay after all images are built, completing the GitOps CI/CD loop.

Purpose: This is the final piece connecting CI image builds to ArgoCD cluster rollouts. After all 3 images are pushed to ghcr.io, the new job updates each per-service overlay's kustomization.yaml with the ghcr.io registry path and SHA tag, then commits and pushes. ArgoCD (configured in Phase 26 with auto-sync) detects the Git change and rolls out updated pods automatically.

Output: Modified container-images.yml with an update-manifests job that closes the GitOps loop: push to master -> build images -> commit image tags -> ArgoCD sync -> new pods.
</objective>

```yaml

jobs:

  build-apiservice:  # existing - builds and pushes apiservice image to ghcr.io
  build-gateway:     # existing - builds and pushes gateway image to ghcr.io
  # NEW: update-manifests job goes here
```

Each build job uses docker/metadata-action@v5 with `type=sha,prefix=sha-` which produces tags like `sha-860c190` (7-character short SHA).

Each build job has:
- `permissions: contents: read, packages: write`

<!-- Current overlay structure -->
Each per-service overlay (apiservice, gateway, web) has this pattern:
```yaml
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization
namespace: micro-commerce
resources:
- ../../../base/{service}
images:
- name: {service}
  newName: {service}
  newTag: dev
```

After the update-manifests job runs, each overlay will look like:
```yaml
images:
- name: {service}
  newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/{service}
  newTag: sha-860c190
```

<!-- ArgoCD sync behavior (from Phase 26) -->
ArgoCD is configured with:

- Self-heal enabled (reverts manual changes)
- 3-minute default polling interval
