<tasks>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Add update-manifests job to container-images.yml</name>
  <files>.github/workflows/container-images.yml</files>
  <action>
    Append a new `update-manifests` job to the existing `.github/workflows/container-images.yml` workflow file. Add it after the `build-gateway` job (at the end of the jobs section).

    The new job definition:
    ```yaml
    update-manifests:
      name: Update Kustomize Manifests
      needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]
      if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'
      runs-on: ubuntu-latest
      permissions:
        contents: write
      steps:
        - name: Checkout
          uses: actions/checkout@v4

        - name: Update image tags in dev overlay
          run: |
            SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
            echo "Updating image tags to: $SHORT_SHA"

            cd infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice
            kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:$SHORT_SHA

            cd ../gateway
            kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/gateway:$SHORT_SHA

            cd ../web
            kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/web:$SHORT_SHA

        - name: Commit and push
          run: |
            git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
            git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
            git add infra/k8s/overlays/dev/
            git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes to commit" && exit 0
            git commit -m "[skip ci] chore: update image tags to sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
            git push
    ```

    Key design decisions:
    1. **`needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]`** -- only runs after ALL 3 images are successfully built and pushed. If any build fails, manifests are not updated.
    2. **`if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'`** -- prevents manifest updates on tag pushes (`v*.*.*`), which are release artifacts, not deployments.
    3. **`permissions: contents: write`** -- job-level permission, scoped only to this job. The existing build jobs keep their `contents: read` + `packages: write`.
    4. **`kustomize edit set image <oldName>=<newName>:<newTag>`** -- updates both `newName` (to ghcr.io registry path) and `newTag` (to SHA) in one command. This is the official Kustomize CLI pattern, not fragile sed.
    5. **Short SHA format: `sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)`** -- matches the exact format produced by `docker/metadata-action`'s `type=sha,prefix=sha-` (7-character short SHA with `sha-` prefix).
    6. **`git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes" && exit 0`** -- prevents empty commits if the workflow is re-run on the same commit (idempotent).
    7. **`[skip ci]` in commit message** -- belt-and-suspenders protection against infinite loops. GITHUB_TOKEN already prevents re-triggering push-based workflows, but `[skip ci]` is an additional safety net.
    8. **No PAT** -- the default GITHUB_TOKEN is used (no explicit token configuration needed). GitHub Actions does not trigger push-based workflows for commits made by GITHUB_TOKEN.

    DO NOT modify any existing job (build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway). Only append the new job.
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd /Users/baotoq/Work/micro-commerce && grep -q "update-manifests:" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "needs: \[build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway\]" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "contents: write" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "\[skip ci\]" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "git diff --staged --quiet" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q 'cut -c1-7' .github/workflows/container-images.yml && echo "ALL CHECKS PASS"</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>
    - update-manifests job added to container-images.yml
    - Job depends on all 3 build jobs (needs clause)
    - Job only runs on master branch pushes (if condition)
    - Job has contents: write permission
    - kustomize edit set image updates all 3 service overlays with ghcr.io registry path + SHA tag

    - SHA format matches docker/metadata-action output (sha- prefix + 7 chars)

  </done>
</task>

## Phase 27 Planning Complete

### Plan Created

### Plan 27-01: Add update-manifests job to container-images.yml

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Wave | 1 |
| Dependencies | None |
| Files modified | `.github/workflows/container-images.yml` |
| Autonomous | true |
| Tasks | 2 |

**Task 1: Add update-manifests job** -- Appends a new GitHub Actions job to the existing `container-images.yml` workflow. The job:
- Depends on all 3 build jobs (`needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]`)
- Only runs on master branch pushes (`if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'`)
- Uses `kustomize edit set image` to update each per-service overlay with `ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/{service}:sha-<7char>` tags
- Commits with `[skip ci]` via `GITHUB_TOKEN` (prevents infinite workflow loops)
- Includes `git diff --staged --quiet` guard against empty commits

**Task 2: Validate workflow YAML and GitOps loop integrity** -- Validates YAML syntax, confirms exactly 4 jobs exist (3 original + 1 new), verifies overlay directory paths, and ensures the complete GitOps chain is correct.

### Quality Gate Checklist

- [x] PLAN.md file created in phase directory
- [x] Valid frontmatter (phase, plan, wave, depends_on, files_modified, autonomous, requirements)
- [x] Tasks are specific and actionable (exact YAML to add, exact commands to run)

- [x] must_haves derived from phase goal (8 truths, 1 artifact, 3 key_links)

### Key Design Decisions in the Plan

1. **Single plan** -- The entire phase is a single workflow file modification (~30 lines of YAML). No need for multiple plans.
2. **No overlay file modifications** -- The plan does NOT modify the overlay `kustomization.yaml` files directly. CI does that at runtime via `kustomize edit set image`.
3. **GITHUB_TOKEN + [skip ci]** -- Belt-and-suspenders infinite loop prevention. GITHUB_TOKEN commits don't trigger push-based workflows, and `[skip ci]` is an additional safety net.
4. **kustomize edit set image** -- The official Kustomize CLI command updates both `newName` (to ghcr.io path) and `newTag` (to SHA) atomically. No fragile sed/awk.

   120→<task type="auto">
   121→  <name>Task 1: Add update-manifests job to container-images.yml</name>
   122→  <files>.github/workflows/container-images.yml</files>
   123→  <action>
   124→    Append a new `update-manifests` job to the existing `.github/workflows/container-images.yml` workflow file. Add it after the `build-gateway` job (at the end of the jobs section).
   125→
   126→    The new job definition:
   127→    ```yaml
   128→    update-manifests:
   129→      name: Update Kustomize Manifests
   130→      needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]
   131→      if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'
   132→      runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   133→      permissions:
   134→        contents: write
   135→      steps:
   136→        - name: Checkout
   137→          uses: actions/checkout@v4
   138→
   139→        - name: Update image tags in dev overlay
   140→          run: |
   141→            SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   142→            echo "Updating image tags to: $SHORT_SHA"
   143→
   144→            cd infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice
   145→            kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:$SHORT_SHA
   146→
   147→            cd ../gateway
   148→            kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/gateway:$SHORT_SHA
   149→
   150→            cd ../web
   151→            kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/web:$SHORT_SHA
   152→
   153→        - name: Commit and push
   154→          run: |
   155→            git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
   156→            git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
   157→            git add infra/k8s/overlays/dev/
   158→            git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes to commit" && exit 0
   159→            git commit -m "[skip ci] chore: update image tags to sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   160→            git push
   161→    ```
   162→
   163→    Key design decisions:
   164→    1. **`needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]`** -- only runs after ALL 3 images are successfully built and pushed. If any build fails, manifests are not updated.
   165→    2. **`if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'`** -- prevents manifest updates on tag pushes (`v*.*.*`), which are release artifacts, not deployments.
   166→    3. **`permissions: contents: write`** -- job-level permission, scoped only to this job. The existing build jobs keep their `contents: read` + `packages: write`.
   167→    4. **`kustomize edit set image <oldName>=<newName>:<newTag>`** -- updates both `newName` (to ghcr.io registry path) and `newTag` (to SHA) in one command. This is the official Kustomize CLI pattern, not fragile sed.
   168→    5. **Short SHA format: `sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)`** -- matches the exact format produced by `docker/metadata-action`'s `type=sha,prefix=sha-` (7-character short SHA with `sha-` prefix).
   169→    6. **`git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes" && exit 0`** -- prevents empty commits if the workflow is re-run on the same commit (idempotent).
   170→    7. **`[skip ci]` in commit message** -- belt-and-suspenders protection against infinite loops. GITHUB_TOKEN already prevents re-triggering push-based workflows, but `[skip ci]` is an additional safety net.
   171→    8. **No PAT** -- the default GITHUB_TOKEN is used (no explicit token configuration needed). GitHub Actions does not trigger push-based workflows for commits made by GITHUB_TOKEN.
   172→
   173→    DO NOT modify any existing job (build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway). Only append the new job.
   174→  </action>

   177→  </verify>
   178→  <done>

   180→    - Job depends on all 3 build jobs (needs clause)
   181→    - Job only runs on master branch pushes (if condition)
   182→    - Job has contents: write permission
   183→    - kustomize edit set image updates all 3 service overlays with ghcr.io registry path + SHA tag
   184→    - git commit uses [skip ci] message
   185→    - git diff --staged --quiet prevents empty commits
   186→    - SHA format matches docker/metadata-action output (sha- prefix + 7 chars)
   187→    - No existing jobs modified
   188→  </done>

    58→### 🚧 v3.0 Kubernetes & GitOps (In Progress)
    59→
    60→**Milestone Goal:** Deploy the full MicroCommerce stack to a kind-based Kubernetes cluster using GitOps via ArgoCD, with GitHub Actions CI building and pushing images to ghcr.io, Kustomize managing manifests, and Sealed Secrets handling credentials safely in Git.
    61→
    62→- [x] **Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline** - Build production-ready images for all three services and push them to ghcr.io via GitHub Actions (completed 2026-02-25)
    63→- [x] **Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets** - Deploy PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak in the kind cluster with Sealed Secrets for all credentials (completed 2026-02-26)
    64→- [x] **Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport** - Deploy ApiService, Gateway, and Web with Kustomize base/overlay structure and RabbitMQ transport support (completed 2026-02-26)
    65→- [x] **Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps** - Install ArgoCD and wire app-of-apps to manage all cluster resources from Git (completed 2026-03-02)
    66→- [ ] **Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure** - Extend CI to commit SHA image tags back to the overlay, completing the full GitOps loop
    67→- [ ] **Phase 28: Observability** - Deploy OTEL Collector and standalone Aspire Dashboard for in-cluster monitoring
    68→
    69→## Phase Details
    70→
    71→### Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline
    72→**Goal**: Three production-ready container images exist in ghcr.io and are built automatically on every push to master
    73→**Depends on**: Phase 22 (v2.0 complete)
    74→**Requirements**: CONT-01, CONT-02, CONT-03, CONT-04, CICD-01
    75→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    76→  1. `docker build` succeeds for ApiService, Gateway, and Web with no warnings or root-user violations
    77→  2. `docker run` starts each service and the process responds to health checks within 30 seconds
    78→  3. A push to master triggers the GitHub Actions workflow and all three images appear in ghcr.io tagged with the commit SHA
    79→  4. Image sizes are reasonable: .NET images use chiseled runtime and Next.js uses standalone output with node:alpine
    80→**Plans**: TBD
    81→
    82→### Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets
    83→**Goal**: PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak run stably in the kind cluster with all credentials sealed in Git
    84→**Depends on**: Phase 23
    85→**Requirements**: INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03, INFRA-04, INFRA-05, GOPS-03
    86→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    87→  1. `kind create cluster --config kind-config.yaml` creates a cluster and all infrastructure pods reach Running state
    88→  2. PostgreSQL data persists across pod restarts (PVC-backed StatefulSet)
    89→  3. Keycloak admin UI is reachable and the MicroCommerce realm is pre-loaded from a ConfigMap
    90→  4. No plaintext credentials exist in any committed YAML file; all secrets are SealedSecret objects
    91→  5. Keycloak startup probe is configured with sufficient timeout (15s delay + 60s probing window) to survive slow first-boot realm import
    92→**Plans**: 4 plans
    93→- [x] 24-01-PLAN.md -- Kind cluster config, Kustomize scaffold, PostgreSQL StatefulSet
    94→- [x] 24-02-PLAN.md -- RabbitMQ Deployment, Keycloak Deployment with realm import
    95→- [x] 24-03-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script with SealedSecrets integration
    96→- [x] 24-04-PLAN.md -- Gap closure: fix ROADMAP misalignment on success criterion #5
    97→
    98→### Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport
    99→**Goal**: ApiService, Gateway, and Web are deployed via Kustomize and communicate correctly using K8s DNS and RabbitMQ messaging
   100→**Depends on**: Phase 24
   101→**Requirements**: MFST-01, MFST-02, MFST-03, MFST-04, MFST-05, MFST-06, TRAN-01, TRAN-02
   102→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   103→  1. The storefront loads in a browser via the Gateway NodePort — browsing products and adding to cart works end-to-end
   104→  2. Checkout completes successfully using the RabbitMQ transport (saga orchestrates stock reservation and mock payment)
   105→  3. All pods report liveness and readiness as healthy (`kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce` shows all Running/Ready)
   106→  4. All resources are in the `micro-commerce` namespace and have CPU/memory resource limits set
   107→  5. Setting `MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT=AzureServiceBus` (Aspire dev path) and `RabbitMQ` (K8s path) both produce working configurations
   108→  6. ApiService startup probe prevents liveness failure during slow first-boot EF Core migration (deferred from Phase 24 INFRA-05)
   109→**Plans**: 3 plans

1→# Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure - Research
     2→
     3→**Researched:** 2026-03-02
     4→**Domain:** GitHub Actions CI/CD, Kustomize image tag automation, ArgoCD GitOps sync
     5→**Confidence:** HIGH
     6→
     7→## Summary
     8→
     9→Phase 27 closes the GitOps loop: a push to `master` triggers the existing `container-images.yml` workflow which builds and pushes SHA-tagged images to ghcr.io, then a new job in that same workflow updates the Kustomize dev overlay `kustomization.yaml` files with the new SHA-based image tags and commits the change back to the repo. ArgoCD (installed in Phase 26 with automated sync + self-heal) detects the Git change and rolls out updated pods automatically.
    10→
    11→The existing infrastructure is well-positioned for this. The `container-images.yml` workflow already builds all three images (apiservice, gateway, web) with `type=sha,prefix=sha-` tags via `docker/metadata-action@v5`, producing tags like `sha-860c190`. The Kustomize dev overlays (`infra/k8s/overlays/dev/{service}/kustomization.yaml`) already have per-service `images:` blocks with `newName` and `newTag` fields. ArgoCD Applications already point to these overlay paths with automated sync enabled. The only missing piece is a GitHub Actions job that updates `newTag` in each overlay's `kustomization.yaml` and commits the change.
    12→
    13→A critical design consideration is infinite loop prevention. When the workflow commits updated image tags back to `master`, it must not re-trigger itself. GitHub Actions has built-in protection: events triggered by `GITHUB_TOKEN` do **not** create new workflow runs (except for `workflow_dispatch` and `repository_dispatch`). Since the `container-images.yml` workflow triggers on `push: branches: [master]`, a commit pushed via `GITHUB_TOKEN` will not re-trigger it. As an additional safety measure, the commit message should include `[skip ci]`.
    14→
    15→**Primary recommendation:** Add an `update-manifests` job to the existing `container-images.yml` workflow that runs after all three build jobs complete. This job checks out the repo, runs `kustomize edit set image` in each per-service overlay directory to update `newTag` to `sha-<short-sha>`, commits the three modified `kustomization.yaml` files, and pushes to `master` using `GITHUB_TOKEN` with `contents: write` permission. The overlay `newName` fields must also change from local names (`apiservice`, `gateway`, `web`) to the full ghcr.io registry paths.
    16→
    17→<phase_requirements>
    18→## Phase Requirements
    19→
    20→| ID | Description | Research Support |
    21→|----|-------------|-----------------|
    22→| CICD-02 | CI commits updated SHA image tags to Kustomize dev overlay, triggering ArgoCD sync | The `update-manifests` job uses `kustomize edit set image` to write `sha-<short-sha>` tags into each per-service overlay kustomization.yaml, commits with `[skip ci]`, and pushes via GITHUB_TOKEN. ArgoCD auto-sync detects the tag change within its 3-minute polling interval (or immediately with a webhook) and rolls out new pods. Every image tag in Git matches the commit SHA that built it, providing full traceability. |
    23→</phase_requirements>
    24→
    25→## Standard Stack
    26→
    27→### Core
    28→| Tool | Version | Purpose | Why Standard |
    29→|------|---------|---------|--------------|
    30→| GitHub Actions | N/A (platform) | CI/CD pipeline; build images + commit manifest updates | Already in use for `container-images.yml`; no new tooling needed |
    31→| Kustomize | built-in (kubectl) | `kustomize edit set image` updates overlay `kustomization.yaml` | Already used in overlays; CLI command is the standard automation pattern for image tag updates |
    32→| docker/metadata-action | v5 | Generates SHA-prefixed image tags (`sha-860c190`) | Already in use in all three build jobs; provides consistent tag format |
    33→| ArgoCD | v3.3.2 | Detects Git changes, syncs cluster state automatically | Installed in Phase 26; auto-sync + self-heal already enabled on all Applications |
    34→
    35→### Supporting
    36→| Tool | Version | Purpose | When to Use |
    37→|------|---------|---------|-------------|
    38→| actions/checkout@v4 | v4 | Checkout repo in the update-manifests job | Required to access and modify kustomization.yaml files |
    39→| git (CLI) | built-in | Commit and push updated kustomization.yaml files | Standard approach; no third-party action needed for simple commit+push |
    40→
    41→### Alternatives Considered
    42→| Instead of | Could Use | Tradeoff |
    43→|------------|-----------|----------|
    44→| `kustomize edit set image` CLI | Direct sed/yq replacement of newTag | `kustomize edit set image` is the official Kustomize CLI command; sed is fragile and breaks on format changes |
    45→| Commit in same workflow | Separate `repository_dispatch` event | Adds complexity; same-workflow job is simpler and runs only when builds succeed |
    46→| `stefanzweifel/git-auto-commit-action` | Manual `git commit && git push` | Third-party action adds a dependency; manual git commands are ~5 lines and fully transparent |
    47→| ArgoCD polling (3 min default) | GitHub webhook to ArgoCD `/api/webhook` | Webhook gives <5s detection but requires ArgoCD to be internet-accessible; kind cluster is local-only. Polling is sufficient for the success criterion (5 min). Can add webhook later for cloud deployments. |
    48→| Short SHA tag (`sha-860c190`) | Full commit SHA tag | Short SHA (7 chars) is the `docker/metadata-action` default with `type=sha,prefix=sha-`; sufficient for uniqueness and traceability. Already used in the existing workflow. |
    49→
    50→**Installation:**
    51→```bash
    52→# No new tools to install. Kustomize is built into the GitHub Actions runner via kubectl.
    53→# If standalone kustomize is needed:
    54→# - uses: imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2
    55→# But kubectl built-in kustomize is sufficient.
    56→```
    57→
    58→## Architecture Patterns
    59→
    60→### Recommended Workflow Structure
    61→```
    62→.github/workflows/container-images.yml
    63→  build-web:         (existing) Build + push web image to ghcr.io
    64→  build-apiservice:  (existing) Build + push apiservice image to ghcr.io
    65→  build-gateway:     (existing) Build + push gateway image to ghcr.io
    66→  update-manifests:  (NEW) Runs after all 3 builds complete
    67→    - Checkout repo
    68→    - kustomize edit set image for each overlay
    69→    - git commit + push [skip ci]
    70→```
    71→
    72→### Pattern 1: Update-Manifests Job with `needs` Dependency
    73→**What:** A GitHub Actions job that depends on all three build jobs (`needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]`), updates Kustomize overlays with the new SHA tag, and commits the change.
    74→**When to use:** When all images in a monorepo are built and deployed together.
    75→**Example:**
    76→```yaml
    77→# Source: Standard GitOps pattern verified via official Kustomize docs + GitHub Actions docs
    78→update-manifests:
    79→  name: Update Kustomize Manifests
    80→  needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]
    81→  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    82→  permissions:
    83→    contents: write
    84→  steps:
    85→    - name: Checkout
    86→      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    87→
    88→    - name: Update image tags in dev overlay
    89→      env:
    90→        SHORT_SHA: sha-${{ github.sha }}  # Will be trimmed to 7 chars below
    91→      run: |
    92→        SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
    93→
    94→        cd infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice
    95→        kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:$SHORT_SHA
    96→
    97→        cd ../gateway
    98→        kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/gateway:$SHORT_SHA
    99→
   100→        cd ../web
   101→        kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/web:$SHORT_SHA
   102→
   103→    - name: Commit and push
   104→      run: |
   105→        git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
   106→        git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
   107→        git add infra/k8s/overlays/dev/
   108→        git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes to commit" && exit 0
   109→        git commit -m "[skip ci] chore: update image tags to sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   110→        git push
   111→```
   112→
   113→### Pattern 2: Overlay Image Name Correction
   114→**What:** The current dev overlay kustomization.yaml files use local-only image names (`apiservice:dev`, `gateway:dev`, `web:dev`) that match the bootstrap script's local builds. For CI/CD, the `newName` must point to the ghcr.io registry path.
   115→**When to use:** When the same overlay serves both local development (kind with local images) and CI/CD (ghcr.io images).
   116→**Key insight:** `kustomize edit set image` with the syntax `<oldName>=<newName>:<newTag>` updates both `newName` and `newTag` in one command. Running `kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190` updates the overlay from:
   117→```yaml
   118→images:
   119→- name: apiservice
   120→  newName: apiservice
   121→  newTag: dev
   122→```
   123→to:
   124→```yaml
   125→images:
   126→- name: apiservice
   127→  newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice
   128→  newTag: sha-860c190
   129→```
   130→This means the base deployment's `image: apiservice:dev` gets transformed by Kustomize to `image: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190`.
   131→
   132→### Pattern 3: SHA Tag Format Alignment
   133→**What:** The `docker/metadata-action` `type=sha,prefix=sha-` generates tags like `sha-860c190` (7-character short SHA). The `update-manifests` job must produce the same format.
   134→**When to use:** Always -- the tag in `kustomization.yaml` must exactly match a tag that exists in ghcr.io.
   135→**Example:**
   136→```bash
   137→# docker/metadata-action produces: sha-860c190 (7 chars)
   138→# To match in shell:
   139→SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   140→# Result: sha-860c190
   141→```
   142→
   143→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   144→- **Using `latest` tag in kustomization.yaml:** Defeats traceability. Every deployment must map to a specific commit SHA.
   145→- **Committing with a PAT instead of GITHUB_TOKEN:** PAT-based commits trigger subsequent workflows, risking infinite loops. GITHUB_TOKEN commits do not trigger push-based workflows.
   146→- **Running `kustomize edit set image` in the base directory:** The base deployment.yaml has the original image reference; the overlay is where environment-specific image overrides belong.
   147→- **Hardcoding the SHA in the workflow:** Use `${{ github.sha }}` and `cut -c1-7` to derive the short SHA dynamically.
   148→- **Skipping the `git diff --staged --quiet` check:** If no files changed (e.g., re-run of the same commit), the commit step should be a no-op rather than failing.
   149→- **Modifying overlay for tag-only push (v\*.\*.\*):** The workflow triggers on both `push: branches: [master]` and `push: tags: ['v*.*.*']`. The manifest update should only happen for branch pushes to `master`, not for tag pushes (which are release artifacts, not deployments).
   150→
   151→## Don't Hand-Roll
   152→
   153→| Problem | Don't Build | Use Instead | Why |
   154→|---------|-------------|-------------|-----|
   155→| Image tag update in YAML | Custom sed/awk to replace `newTag:` | `kustomize edit set image` | Handles YAML formatting, multi-line values, comments; official CLI command |
   156→| Git commit automation | Custom shell script with error handling | `git config && git add && git commit && git push` in workflow step | Simple enough for 5 lines; no third-party action needed |
   157→| SHA tag generation | Custom hashing or tag derivation | `docker/metadata-action` `type=sha,prefix=sha-` | Already generates correct format; just derive matching format in update job |
   158→| ArgoCD sync trigger | Custom API call to ArgoCD | ArgoCD's built-in Git polling (3 min) | Polling is sufficient for 5-minute SLA; webhook requires internet-accessible ArgoCD |
   159→
   160→**Key insight:** The entire GitOps loop closure is ~30 lines of workflow YAML added to an existing file. No new tools, no new repos, no complex infrastructure. The hard work was done in Phases 23-26.
   161→
   162→## Common Pitfalls
   163→
   164→### Pitfall 1: Infinite Workflow Loop
   165→**What goes wrong:** The `update-manifests` job commits to `master`, which triggers `container-images.yml` again, which builds images and commits again, forever.
   166→**Why it happens:** If using a PAT or misconfigured token, push events can re-trigger the same workflow.
   167→**How to avoid:** Use `GITHUB_TOKEN` (not a PAT) for the git push. GitHub Actions does not trigger `push`-based workflows for commits made by `GITHUB_TOKEN`. As a belt-and-suspenders measure, include `[skip ci]` in the commit message.
   168→**Warning signs:** Multiple identical workflow runs in quick succession; the Actions tab shows a cascade of runs.
   169→
   170→### Pitfall 2: SHA Tag Mismatch
   171→**What goes wrong:** The tag in `kustomization.yaml` (e.g., `sha-860c190`) does not match any tag pushed to ghcr.io, causing `ImagePullBackOff` in the cluster.
   172→**Why it happens:** The `docker/metadata-action` uses a 7-character short SHA by default, but the update-manifests job uses a different length or format.
   173→**How to avoid:** Use `echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7` to produce the same 7-character short SHA. Verify by checking `docker/metadata-action` step output in a previous run.
   174→**Warning signs:** Pods in `ImagePullBackOff`; `kubectl describe pod` shows "manifest unknown" or "not found" for the image tag.
   175→
   176→### Pitfall 3: Race Condition on Push
   177→**What goes wrong:** The `update-manifests` job checks out the repo at the triggering commit, but another commit lands on `master` before the push, causing a `push rejected (non-fast-forward)` error.
   178→**Why it happens:** In a high-traffic repo, multiple pushes to `master` can overlap.
   179→**How to avoid:** For this project (single developer), this is extremely unlikely. If it becomes an issue, add `git pull --rebase` before `git push`, or use a retry step. The workflow will simply fail and the next push will succeed.
   180→**Warning signs:** `update-manifests` job fails with "non-fast-forward" error.
   181→
   182→### Pitfall 4: Tag Push Triggers Manifest Update
   183→**What goes wrong:** A release tag push (`v1.0.0`) triggers the workflow, and the `update-manifests` job updates overlays with the SHA of the tag commit, overwriting the latest `master` SHA tag.
   184→**Why it happens:** The workflow triggers on both `push: branches: [master]` and `push: tags: ['v*.*.*']`.
   185→**How to avoid:** Add an `if` condition to the `update-manifests` job: `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'`. This ensures manifest updates only happen for branch pushes, not tag pushes.
   186→**Warning signs:** Overlay kustomization.yaml being updated on tag pushes with non-master SHAs.
   187→
   188→### Pitfall 5: Overlay `newName` Still Points to Local Image
   189→**What goes wrong:** The overlay has `newName: apiservice` (local Docker name) instead of `newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice` (registry path). ArgoCD syncs the manifest, but the pod tries to pull `apiservice:sha-860c190` from Docker Hub instead of ghcr.io.
   190→**Why it happens:** The current overlays were set up for local kind development where images are loaded directly. CI/CD images live in ghcr.io.
   191→**How to avoid:** `kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-xxx` updates both `newName` and `newTag` simultaneously. This is the correct command to use.
   192→**Warning signs:** Pods in `ImagePullBackOff` with "repository does not exist" error.
   193→
   194→### Pitfall 6: Bootstrap Script Conflicts with CI/CD Overlays
   195→**What goes wrong:** The bootstrap script builds local images tagged `dev` and the overlays reference `newTag: dev`. After CI/CD updates the overlay to `sha-xxx`, the bootstrap script no longer works because the local kind cluster doesn't have `ghcr.io/...` images.
   196→**Why it happens:** The overlay serves two purposes: local development and CI/CD deployment.
   197→**How to avoid:** Accept that the overlay in Git reflects the last CI/CD deployment. For local development, the bootstrap script can either: (a) run `kustomize edit set image` to reset overlays to local tags after applying, or (b) be updated to also tag local images with the same format. The simplest approach is to accept that the overlay reflects CI/CD state and the bootstrap script's local image loading works independently (ArgoCD is not used in local kind development without the full CI/CD pipeline).
   198→**Warning signs:** Bootstrap script succeeds but ArgoCD shows OutOfSync because overlay tags don't match loaded images.
   199→
   200→## Code Examples
   201→
   202→Verified patterns from official sources:
   203→
   204→### Complete `update-manifests` Job
   205→```yaml
   206→# Source: Standard GitOps pattern from Kustomize + GitHub Actions docs
   207→update-manifests:
   208→  name: Update Kustomize Manifests
   209→  needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]
   210→  if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'
   211→  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   212→  permissions:
   213→    contents: write
   214→  steps:
   215→    - name: Checkout
   216→      uses: actions/checkout@v4
   217→
   218→    - name: Update image tags in dev overlay
   219→      run: |
   220→        SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   221→        echo "Updating image tags to: $SHORT_SHA"
   222→
   223→        cd infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice
   224→        kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:$SHORT_SHA
   225→
   226→        cd ../gateway
   227→        kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/gateway:$SHORT_SHA
   228→
   229→        cd ../web
   230→        kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/web:$SHORT_SHA
   231→
   232→    - name: Commit and push
   233→      run: |
   234→        git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
   235→        git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
   236→        git add infra/k8s/overlays/dev/
   237→        git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes to commit" && exit 0
   238→        git commit -m "[skip ci] chore: update image tags to sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   239→        git push
   240→```
   241→
   242→### Kustomize Edit Set Image Command
   243→```bash
   244→# Source: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/blob/master/examples/image.md
   245→# Syntax: kustomize edit set image <oldName>=<newRegistry>/<newName>:<newTag>
   246→
   247→# Before (in kustomization.yaml):
   248→# images:
   249→# - name: apiservice
   250→#   newName: apiservice
   251→#   newTag: dev
   252→
   253→kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190
   254→
   255→# After (in kustomization.yaml):
   256→# images:
   257→# - name: apiservice
   258→#   newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice
   259→#   newTag: sha-860c190
   260→```
   261→
   262→### SHA Tag Derivation
   263→```bash
   264→# docker/metadata-action with type=sha,prefix=sha- produces: sha-860c190 (7 chars)
   265→# To match in shell:
   266→SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   267→# For full SHA: ${{ github.sha }} = 860c1904a1ce19322e91ac35af1ab07466440c37
   268→# Short: sha-860c190
   269→```
   270→
   271→### Verifying the Loop End-to-End
   272→```bash
   273→# 1. Check the latest image tag in the overlay
   274→cat infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice/kustomization.yaml
   275→# images:
   276→# - name: apiservice
   277→#   newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice
   278→#   newTag: sha-860c190
   279→
   280→# 2. Check ArgoCD sees the updated Application
   281→kubectl get applications -n argocd -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.status.sync.status}{"\n"}{end}'
   282→
   283→# 3. Check the running pod's image matches
   284→kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.spec.containers[0].image}{"\n"}{end}'
   285→# apiservice-xxx   ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190
   286→```
   287→
   288→## State of the Art
   289→
   290→| Old Approach | Current Approach | When Changed | Impact |
   291→|--------------|------------------|--------------|--------|
   292→| Separate config repo for manifests | Single repo (app + infra in same repo) | Ongoing pattern | Simpler for single-team projects; `GITHUB_TOKEN` can commit to same repo |
   293→| Custom webhook handlers for ArgoCD | ArgoCD built-in 3-min polling or `/api/webhook` | ArgoCD 1.x+ | No custom infrastructure needed for GitOps sync trigger |
   294→| `sed -i` to replace image tags | `kustomize edit set image` CLI | Kustomize 3.x+ | YAML-aware, handles formatting, official tool |
   295→| PAT-based commits in CI | GITHUB_TOKEN with `contents: write` | GitHub Actions 2021+ | Built-in infinite loop prevention; no token management overhead |
   296→
   297→**Deprecated/outdated:**
   298→- Using `sed` to modify Kustomize files: fragile, not YAML-aware
   299→- Separate GitOps repository for manifests: adds complexity for single-team projects; single-repo is standard for small projects
   300→- ArgoCD Image Updater (write-back): designed for auto-updating to latest tags; not needed when CI explicitly sets the tag
   301→
   302→## Open Questions
   303→
   304→1. **Bootstrap script compatibility after CI/CD overlay update**
   305→   - What we know: The bootstrap script builds local images tagged `dev` and uses overlays with `newTag: dev`. After CI/CD, the overlay will have `newTag: sha-xxx` pointing to ghcr.io.
   306→   - What's unclear: Should the bootstrap script be updated to handle this divergence, or is it acceptable that the overlay in Git reflects CI/CD state?
   307→   - Recommendation: Accept the divergence. The bootstrap script's Step 10-11 (build/load images) and Step 12 (apply ArgoCD root app) work independently. For local development, the user can either: (a) let the bootstrap script work as-is (ArgoCD will sync from Git, pulling ghcr.io images if the cluster has internet), or (b) after bootstrap, manually reset overlays with `kustomize edit set image apiservice=apiservice:dev` etc. This is a local dev workflow concern, not a CI/CD concern. Defer to implementation.
   308→
   309→2. **ArgoCD sync latency vs 5-minute SLA**
   310→   - What we know: ArgoCD polls Git every 3 minutes by default. The success criterion requires a new pod within 5 minutes of CI workflow completing.
   311→   - What's unclear: Whether 3-minute polling + pod rollout time fits within 5 minutes.
   312→   - Recommendation: 3-minute polling + ~30s for pod rollout = ~3.5 minutes typical, well within the 5-minute SLA. If tighter latency is needed later, configure a GitHub webhook to ArgoCD's `/api/webhook` endpoint. Not needed for this phase.
   313→
   314→3. **`contents: write` permission scope**
   315→   - What we know: The `update-manifests` job needs `permissions: contents: write` to push commits. The build jobs currently have `permissions: contents: read, packages: write`.
   316→   - What's unclear: Whether job-level permissions can differ from workflow-level permissions.
   317→   - Recommendation: GitHub Actions supports per-job permissions. Set `contents: write` only on the `update-manifests` job. The build jobs keep their existing `contents: read` + `packages: write`.
   318→
   319→## Validation Architecture
   320→
   321→### Test Framework
   322→| Property | Value |
   323→|----------|-------|
   324→| Framework | Manual validation via GitHub Actions run + kubectl (infrastructure/CI phase, no unit test framework) |
   325→| Config file | `.github/workflows/container-images.yml` |
   326→| Quick run command | Check workflow run status in GitHub Actions tab |
   327→| Full suite command | Push to master, verify: (1) workflow completes, (2) overlay kustomization.yaml updated in Git, (3) ArgoCD syncs, (4) pods running new image |
   328→
   329→### Phase Requirements to Test Map
   330→| Req ID | Behavior | Test Type | Automated Command | File Exists? |
   331→|--------|----------|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
   332→| CICD-02 | CI commits updated SHA image tags to Kustomize dev overlay | smoke | `git log --oneline -1 -- infra/k8s/overlays/dev/` (verify [skip ci] commit) | N/A -- Git history |
   333→| CICD-02 | Image tag in overlay matches commit SHA | smoke | `grep newTag infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice/kustomization.yaml` (verify sha-XXX format) | N/A -- file content |
   334→| CICD-02 | ArgoCD syncs updated manifests | smoke | `kubectl get applications -n argocd -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.sync.status}'` | N/A -- kubectl |
   335→| CICD-02 | Pod runs image matching commit SHA | smoke | `kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[0].image}'` | N/A -- kubectl |
   336→| CICD-02 | Workflow does not trigger infinite loop | smoke | Check GitHub Actions tab shows no cascading runs after the [skip ci] commit | N/A -- GitHub UI |
   337→
   338→### Sampling Rate
   339→- **Per task commit:** Verify workflow YAML syntax with `act --dryrun` or visual review
   340→- **Per wave merge:** Push to master, verify full loop: build -> commit -> ArgoCD sync -> pod update
   341→- **Phase gate:** Both success criteria verified before `/gsd:verify-work`
   342→
   343→### Wave 0 Gaps
   344→None -- this phase modifies a GitHub Actions workflow file and Kustomize overlays. No test framework infrastructure needed.
   345→
   346→## Sources
   347→
   348→### Primary (HIGH confidence)
   349→- [/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/blob/master/examples/image.md) - `kustomize edit set image` command syntax and behavior verified via Context7
   350→- [docker/metadata-action@v5](https://github.com/docker/metadata-action) - SHA tag format: `type=sha,prefix=sha-` produces `sha-<7chars>` by default
   351→- [GitHub Actions: Skipping workflow runs](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/managing-workflow-runs-and-deployments/managing-workflow-runs/skipping-workflow-runs) - `[skip ci]` in commit message skips push/PR-triggered workflows
   352→- [GitHub Actions: GITHUB_TOKEN](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/25702) - Events triggered by GITHUB_TOKEN do not create new workflow runs (except workflow_dispatch/repository_dispatch)
   353→- [ArgoCD Git Webhook Configuration](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/latest/operator-manual/webhook/) - Webhook endpoint at `/api/webhook` for immediate sync (optional, not needed for this phase)
   354→- [ArgoCD Auto Sync](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user-guide/auto_sync/) - Default 3-minute polling interval; selfHeal and automated sync configuration
   355→
   356→### Secondary (MEDIUM confidence)
   357→- [GitOps CI/CD patterns with GitHub Actions + ArgoCD](https://medium.com/@mehmetkanus17/argocd-github-actions-a-complete-gitops-ci-cd-workflow-for-kubernetes-applications-ed2f91d37641) - Workflow structure with `needs:` dependency for update-manifest job
   358→- [Automation with GitHub Actions and Kustomize](https://gap.gjensidige.io/docs/guides/ci-automation-with-kustomize) - `kustomize edit set image` in CI/CD pipelines pattern
   359→
   360→### Tertiary (LOW confidence)
   361→- None -- all findings verified with official documentation
   362→
   363→## Metadata
   364→
   365→**Confidence breakdown:**
   366→- Standard stack: HIGH - No new tools needed; existing `container-images.yml`, Kustomize, and ArgoCD are all already in place. Only addition is a new job in an existing workflow.
   367→- Architecture: HIGH - The `update-manifests` job pattern is the standard GitOps CI/CD pattern, verified across multiple official and community sources. `kustomize edit set image` syntax verified via Context7.
   368→- Pitfalls: HIGH - Infinite loop prevention via GITHUB_TOKEN is documented by GitHub. SHA format alignment is straightforward. Race condition is theoretical for single-developer repo.
   369→
   370→**Research date:** 2026-03-02
   371→**Valid until:** 2026-04-02 (30 days -- GitHub Actions and Kustomize are stable; ArgoCD sync behavior is well-established)

content.text:
I now have all the information needed for a thorough verification. Let me analyze each dimension.

---

## ISSUES FOUND

**Phase:** 27-cicd-gitops-loop-closure
**Plans checked:** 1 (27-01-PLAN.md)
**Issues:** 1 blocker, 1 warning, 0 info

---

### Dimension 1: Requirement Coverage — PASS

The single requirement CICD-02 is listed in the plan's `requirements` frontmatter field and both tasks in the plan directly address it. The requirement definition ("CI commits updated SHA image tags to Kustomize dev overlay, triggering ArgoCD sync") maps to the entire objective of the plan.

| Requirement | Plans | Tasks | Status |
|-------------|-------|-------|--------|
| CICD-02 | 27-01 | 1, 2 | Covered |

---

### Dimension 2: Task Completeness — PASS

| Task | Files | Action | Verify | Done | Status |
|------|-------|--------|--------|------|--------|
| Task 1 | Yes | Yes (specific) | Yes (automated grep chain) | Yes (8-point checklist) | Valid |
| Task 2 | Yes | Yes (specific 4-step validation) | Yes (python3 yaml + dir checks) | Yes (5-point checklist) | Valid |

Both tasks are `type="auto"` and have all four required elements with appropriately specific content.

---

### Dimension 3: Dependency Correctness — PASS

The plan has `depends_on: []`, placing it in Wave 1. There is only one plan in this phase. No cycles, no missing references. Wave assignment is consistent.

---

### Dimension 4: Key Links Planned — BLOCKER

**Critical gap: SHA tag format mismatch between the actual workflow and the plan's assumed format.**

The plan's `must_haves.truths` state:

> "The SHA tag format matches docker/metadata-action output: sha- prefix + 7-character short SHA"

The plan's `action` in Task 1 derives the tag as:

```bash
SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
```

This produces `sha-XXXXXXX` (7-character short SHA). The plan correctly documents this should match `docker/metadata-action`'s output.

However, examining the **actual `container-images.yml` workflow**, the `build-apiservice` and `build-gateway` jobs do NOT use `docker/build-push-action` with `metadata-action` tags for the final pushed manifest. They use:

```yaml
- name: Publish amd64 image
  run: dotnet publish ... -p ContainerImageTags='"${{ github.sha }}-amd64"'

- name: Create and push multi-arch manifest
  run: docker buildx imagetools create $TAG_ARGS \
    ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:${{ github.sha }}-amd64 \
    ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:${{ github.sha }}-arm64
```

The `$TAG_ARGS` is built from `steps.meta.outputs.tags`, which includes `sha-<7chars>` (from `type=sha,prefix=sha-`). So the multi-arch manifest DOES get the `sha-<7chars>` tag applied via `imagetools create`. The `metadata-action` SHA tag IS applied to the multi-arch manifest for apiservice and gateway.

For `build-web`, the standard `docker/build-push-action` with `tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}` is used, which also produces `sha-<7chars>`.

The key links are correctly planned. The format alignment is valid.

**However, there is a real gap: the `kustomize` CLI is assumed to be pre-installed on `ubuntu-latest` GitHub Actions runners, but this is not guaranteed.** The plan states in the RESEARCH.md: "Kustomize is built into the GitHub Actions runner via kubectl." This is accurate — `kubectl` bundles `kubectl kustomize`, but the standalone `kustomize` CLI binary is a separate tool. The plan calls `kustomize edit set image` (standalone CLI), not `kubectl kustomize edit`. The GitHub Actions `ubuntu-latest` runner does NOT ship with the standalone `kustomize` binary pre-installed.

```yaml
issue:
  plan: "27-01"
  dimension: key_links_planned
  severity: blocker
  description: "Task 1 calls 'kustomize edit set image' but the standalone kustomize CLI is not pre-installed on ubuntu-latest runners. kubectl kustomize (built-in) does not support the 'edit' subcommand. Without kustomize being installed, the update-manifests job will fail with 'kustomize: command not found'."
  task: 1
  fix_hint: "Add a kustomize install step before the 'Update image tags in dev overlay' step. Use 'uses: imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2' (listed in RESEARCH.md alternatives) or add a curl install step: 'curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/master/hack/install_kustomize.sh | bash && sudo mv kustomize /usr/local/bin/'. Alternatively, replace 'kustomize edit set image' with 'kubectl kustomize edit set image' — but 'kubectl kustomize' does not support the edit subcommand in older kubectl versions, making the setup-kustomize approach more reliable."
```

---

### Dimension 5: Scope Sanity — PASS

| Plan | Tasks | Files Modified | Wave | Status |
|------|-------|----------------|------|--------|
| 27-01 | 2 | 1 | 1 | Well within budget |

2 tasks against a single workflow file. Task 2 is a validation task on the same file. Well within thresholds.

---

### Dimension 6: Verification Derivation — WARNING

The `must_haves.truths` are largely implementation-focused rather than user-observable:

- "An update-manifests job exists in container-images.yml..." — implementation detail
- "The update-manifests job only runs on master branch pushes..." — implementation detail
- "The update-manifests job has permissions: contents: write" — implementation detail
- "kustomize edit set image updates each per-service overlay..." — implementation detail
- "The git commit message includes [skip ci]..." — implementation detail
- "The git push uses the default GITHUB_TOKEN..." — implementation detail
- "A git diff --staged --quiet check prevents empty commits..." — implementation detail
- "The SHA tag format matches docker/metadata-action output..." — implementation detail

None of the truths are expressed as user- or operator-observable outcomes. The phase goal is "A push to master automatically flows through CI image build, Git tag commit, and ArgoCD cluster rollout without manual intervention." Observable truths should include things like "the overlay kustomization.yaml in Git has sha-XXXXXXX tags after a CI run" or "pods run the image matching the commit SHA."

This is a warning, not a blocker, because the implementation-focused truths are all correct and checkable — they just describe mechanism rather than outcome.

```yaml
issue:
  plan: "27-01"
  dimension: verification_derivation
  severity: warning
  description: "All must_haves.truths are implementation details of the workflow job, not operator-observable outcomes. Phase goal is about the full GitOps loop closing, but no truth captures 'overlay kustomization.yaml shows sha-XXXXXXX after CI run' or 'pods run the image matching the triggering commit SHA'."
  problematic_truths:
    - "The update-manifests job has permissions: contents: write"
    - "The git push uses the default GITHUB_TOKEN (no PAT) for infinite loop prevention"
  fix_hint: "Add at least one operator-observable truth: 'After a push to master, infra/k8s/overlays/dev/*/kustomization.yaml shows newTag: sha-<7chars> matching the triggering commit' and 'No subsequent workflow run is triggered by the [skip ci] commit'. The existing implementation-focused truths can remain as verification points."
```

---

### Dimension 7: Context Compliance — SKIPPED

No CONTEXT.md exists for this phase.

---

### Dimension 8: Nyquist Compliance

The RESEARCH.md `Validation Architecture` section explicitly states:

> Framework: Manual validation via GitHub Actions run + kubectl (infrastructure/CI phase, no unit test framework)

And:

> Wave 0 Gaps: None — this phase modifies a GitHub Actions workflow file and Kustomize overlays. No test framework infrastructure needed.

This is a CI/CD infrastructure phase where automated pre-execution tests do not apply — the verification necessarily happens by running the workflow in GitHub Actions. The plan's `<verify><automated>` commands in Task 1 and Task 2 are static grep/python checks on the file content, which is the correct approach for a workflow YAML modification.

The RESEARCH.md `Validation Architecture` documents the phase-level validation map and explicitly calls out no unit test framework applies. Nyquist validation does not apply to GitHub Actions workflow modifications.

**Dimension 8: SKIPPED (infrastructure/CI phase — validation via GitHub Actions run, no unit test framework; explicitly documented in RESEARCH.md Validation Architecture)**

---

### Blockers (must fix)

**1. [key_links_planned] standalone kustomize CLI not installed on ubuntu-latest runners**
- Plan: 27-01
- Task: 1
- Fix: Add `uses: imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2` (already referenced in RESEARCH.md as a known alternative) as a step before "Update image tags in dev overlay". The RESEARCH.md states "If standalone kustomize is needed: uses: imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2" — this condition is always true since `ubuntu-latest` does not include the standalone `kustomize` binary. The plan omits this required installation step.

### Warnings (should fix)

**1. [verification_derivation] must_haves.truths are all implementation-focused, none are operator-observable outcomes**
- Plan: 27-01
- Fix: Add outcome-level truths reflecting the GitOps loop closing observable state (overlay files updated in Git with SHA tags, no cascading workflow runs triggered).

---

### Structured Issues

```yaml
issues:
  - plan: "27-01"
    dimension: key_links_planned
    severity: blocker
    description: "Task 1 calls 'kustomize edit set image' but the standalone kustomize CLI binary is not pre-installed on ubuntu-latest GitHub Actions runners. The ubuntu-latest runner ships with kubectl but not the standalone kustomize CLI. 'kubectl kustomize' does not support the 'edit' subcommand — it is read-only. The job will fail with 'kustomize: command not found' at runtime."
    task: 1
    fix_hint: "Add a kustomize install step to the update-manifests job before the 'Update image tags in dev overlay' step. RESEARCH.md already documents this option: 'uses: imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2'. Add it as a step: '- name: Setup Kustomize\\n  uses: imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2'. No version pin needed as v2 is latest stable."

  - plan: "27-01"
    dimension: verification_derivation
    severity: warning
    description: "All 8 must_haves.truths are implementation details about workflow YAML structure. None capture the operator-observable outcome: that the overlay files in Git are updated with SHA tags after a CI run, or that no infinite loop occurs. The phase goal ('push to master automatically flows through...without manual intervention') is not reflected in any truth."
    fix_hint: "Add operator-observable truths such as: 'After a push to master, each infra/k8s/overlays/dev/*/kustomization.yaml in Git shows newTag: sha-<7chars> matching the triggering commit SHA' and 'The [skip ci] commit does not trigger a subsequent container-images.yml workflow run'."
```

### Recommendation

1 blocker requires revision. The plan is otherwise sound — requirement coverage is complete, tasks are well-structured with specific actions and automated verification, dependency graph is valid, scope is well within budget, and the technical approach is correct and well-researched. The single blocker is a missing `setup-kustomize` installation step that would cause a runtime failure in GitHub Actions. The fix is one additional step (3 lines of YAML) using a tool already identified in RESEARCH.md.

   144→            cd infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice

   146→
   147→            cd ../gateway
   148→            kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/gateway:$SHORT_SHA
   149→
   150→            cd ../web
   151→            kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/web:$SHORT_SHA

   153→        - name: Commit and push

   182→    - Job has contents: write permission

   200→

   202→       - The workflow trigger includes `push: branches: [master]` (existing)
   203→       - All 3 build jobs use `docker/metadata-action@v5` with `type=sha,prefix=sha-` (existing)
   204→       - The update-manifests job has correct `needs` referencing all 3 build job names
   205→       - The update-manifests job uses `kustomize edit set image` with the format `{service}=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/{service}:$SHORT_SHA` for each of: apiservice, gateway, web
   206→       - The SHORT_SHA derivation uses `cut -c1-7` to match docker/metadata-action's 7-char format
   207→       - The commit message contains `[skip ci]`
   208→       - No `secrets.` references appear in the update-manifests job (uses only GITHUB_TOKEN implicitly via git push)

     8→     8→<role>
     9→     9→You are a GSD planner. You create executable phase plans with task breakdown, dependency analysis, and goal-backward verification.
    10→    10→
    11→    11→Spawned by:
    12→    12→- `/gsd:plan-phase` orchestrator (standard phase planning)
    13→    13→- `/gsd:plan-phase --gaps` orchestrator (gap closure from verification failures)
    14→    14→- `/gsd:plan-phase` in revision mode (updating plans based on checker feedback)
    15→    15→
    16→    16→Your job: Produce PLAN.md files that Claude executors can implement without interpretation. Plans are prompts, not documents that become prompts.
    17→    17→
    18→    18→**CRITICAL: Mandatory Initial Read**
    19→    19→If the prompt contains a `<files_to_read>` block, you MUST use the `Read` tool to load every file listed there before performing any other actions. This is your primary context.
    20→    20→
    21→    21→**Core responsibilities:**
    22→    22→- **FIRST: Parse and honor user decisions from CONTEXT.md** (locked decisions are NON-NEGOTIABLE)
    23→    23→- Decompose phases into parallel-optimized plans with 2-3 tasks each
    24→    24→- Build dependency graphs and assign execution waves
    25→    25→- Derive must-haves using goal-backward methodology
    26→    26→- Handle both standard planning and gap closure mode
    27→    27→- Revise existing plans based on checker feedback (revision mode)
    28→    28→- Return structured results to orchestrator
    29→    29→</role>
    30→    30→
    31→    31→<project_context>
    32→    32→Before planning, discover project context:
    33→    33→
    34→    34→**Project instructions:** Read `./CLAUDE.md` if it exists in the working directory. Follow all project-specific guidelines, security requirements, and coding conventions.
    35→    35→
    36→    36→**Project skills:** Check `.claude/skills/` or `.agents/skills/` directory if either exists:
    37→    37→1. List available skills (subdirectories)
    38→    38→2. Read `SKILL.md` for each skill (lightweight index ~130 lines)
    39→    39→3. Load specific `rules/*.md` files as needed during planning
    40→    40→4. Do NOT load full `AGENTS.md` files (100KB+ context cost)
    41→    41→5. Ensure plans account for project skill patterns and conventions
    42→    42→
    43→    43→This ensures task actions reference the correct patterns and libraries for this project.

    46→    46→<context_fidelity>
    47→    47→## CRITICAL: User Decision Fidelity
    48→    48→
    49→    49→The orchestrator provides user decisions in `<user_decisions>` tags from `/gsd:discuss-phase`.
    50→    50→
    51→    51→**Before creating ANY task, verify:**
    52→    52→
    53→    53→1. **Locked Decisions (from `## Decisions`)** — MUST be implemented exactly as specified
    54→    54→   - If user said "use library X" → task MUST use library X, not an alternative
    55→    55→   - If user said "card layout" → task MUST implement cards, not tables
    56→    56→   - If user said "no animations" → task MUST NOT include animations
    57→    57→
    58→    58→2. **Deferred Ideas (from `## Deferred Ideas`)** — MUST NOT appear in plans
    59→    59→   - If user deferred "search functionality" → NO search tasks allowed
    60→    60→   - If user deferred "dark mode" → NO dark mode tasks allowed
    61→    61→
    62→    62→3. **Claude's Discretion (from `## Claude's Discretion`)** — Use your judgment
    63→    63→   - Make reasonable choices and document in task actions
    64→    64→
    65→    65→**Self-check before returning:** For each plan, verify:
    66→    66→- [ ] Every locked decision has a task implementing it
    67→    67→- [ ] No task implements a deferred idea
    68→    68→- [ ] Discretion areas are handled reasonably
    69→    69→
    70→    70→**If conflict exists** (e.g., research suggests library Y but user locked library X):
    71→    71→- Honor the user's locked decision
    72→    72→- Note in task action: "Using X per user decision (research suggested Y)"
    73→    73→</context_fidelity>
    74→    74→
    75→    75→<philosophy>
    76→    76→
    77→    77→## Solo Developer + Claude Workflow
    78→    78→
    79→    79→Planning for ONE person (the user) and ONE implementer (Claude).
    80→    80→- No teams, stakeholders, ceremonies, coordination overhead
    81→    81→- User = visionary/product owner, Claude = builder
    82→    82→- Estimate effort in Claude execution time, not human dev time
    83→    83→
    84→    84→## Plans Are Prompts
    85→    85→
    86→    86→PLAN.md IS the prompt (not a document that becomes one). Contains:
    87→    87→- Objective (what and why)
    88→    88→- Context (@file references)
    89→    89→- Tasks (with verification criteria)
    90→    90→- Success criteria (measurable)
    91→    91→
    92→    92→## Quality Degradation Curve
    93→    93→
    94→    94→| Context Usage | Quality | Claude's State |
    95→    95→|---------------|---------|----------------|
    96→    96→| 0-30% | PEAK | Thorough, comprehensive |
    97→    97→| 30-50% | GOOD | Confident, solid work |
    98→    98→| 50-70% | DEGRADING | Efficiency mode begins |
    99→    99→| 70%+ | POOR | Rushed, minimal |
   100→   100→
   101→   101→**Rule:** Plans should complete within ~50% context. More plans, smaller scope, consistent quality. Each plan: 2-3 tasks max.
   102→   102→
   103→   103→## Ship Fast
   104→   104→
   105→   105→Plan -> Execute -> Ship -> Learn -> Repeat
   106→   106→
   107→   107→**Anti-enterprise patterns (delete if seen):**
   108→   108→- Team structures, RACI matrices, stakeholder management
   109→   109→- Sprint ceremonies, change management processes
   110→   110→- Human dev time estimates (hours, days, weeks)
   111→   111→- Documentation for documentation's sake
   112→   112→
   113→   113→</philosophy>
   114→   114→
   115→   115→<discovery_levels>
   116→   116→
   117→   117→## Mandatory Discovery Protocol
   118→   118→
   119→   119→Discovery is MANDATORY unless you can prove current context exists.
   120→   120→
   121→   121→**Level 0 - Skip** (pure internal work, existing patterns only)
   122→   122→- ALL work follows established codebase patterns (grep confirms)
   123→   123→- No new external dependencies
   124→   124→- Examples: Add delete button, add field to model, create CRUD endpoint
   125→   125→
   126→   126→**Level 1 - Quick Verification** (2-5 min)
   127→   127→- Single known library, confirming syntax/version
   128→   128→- Action: Context7 resolve-library-id + query-docs, no DISCOVERY.md needed
   129→   129→
   130→   130→**Level 2 - Standard Research** (15-30 min)
   131→   131→- Choosing between 2-3 options, new external integration
   132→   132→- Action: Route to discovery workflow, produces DISCOVERY.md
   133→   133→
   134→   134→**Level 3 - Deep Dive** (1+ hour)
   135→   135→- Architectural decision with long-term impact, novel problem
   136→   136→- Action: Full research with DISCOVERY.md
   137→   137→
   138→   138→**Depth indicators:**
   139→   139→- Level 2+: New library not in package.json, external API, "choose/select/evaluate" in description
   140→   140→- Level 3: "architecture/design/system", multiple external services, data modeling, auth design
   141→   141→
   142→   142→For niche domains (3D, games, audio, shaders, ML), suggest `/gsd:research-phase` before plan-phase.
   143→   143→
   144→   144→</discovery_levels>
   145→   145→
   146→   146→<task_breakdown>
   147→   147→
   148→   148→## Task Anatomy
   149→   149→
   150→   150→Every task has four required fields:
   151→   151→
   152→   152→**<files>:** Exact file paths created or modified.
   153→   153→- Good: `src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts`, `prisma/schema.prisma`
   154→   154→- Bad: "the auth files", "relevant components"
   155→   155→
   156→   156→**<action>:** Specific implementation instructions, including what to avoid and WHY.
   157→   157→- Good: "Create POST endpoint accepting {email, password}, validates using bcrypt against User table, returns JWT in httpOnly cookie with 15-min expiry. Use jose library (not jsonwebtoken - CommonJS issues with Edge runtime)."
   158→   158→- Bad: "Add authentication", "Make login work"
   159→   159→
   160→   160→**<verify>:** How to prove the task is complete.
   161→   161→
   162→   162→```xml
   163→   163→<verify>
   164→   164→  <automated>pytest tests/test_module.py::test_behavior -x</automated>
   165→   165→</verify>
   166→   166→```
   167→   167→
   168→   168→- Good: Specific automated command that runs in < 60 seconds
   169→   169→- Bad: "It works", "Looks good", manual-only verification
   170→   170→- Simple format also accepted: `npm test` passes, `curl -X POST /api/auth/login` returns 200
   171→   171→
   172→   172→**Nyquist Rule:** Every `<verify>` must include an `<automated>` command. If no test exists yet, set `<automated>MISSING — Wave 0 must create {test_file} first</automated>` and create a Wave 0 task that generates the test scaffold.
   173→   173→
   174→   174→**<done>:** Acceptance criteria - measurable state of completion.
   175→   175→- Good: "Valid credentials return 200 + JWT cookie, invalid credentials return 401"
   176→   176→- Bad: "Authentication is complete"
   177→   177→
   178→   178→## Task Types
   179→   179→
   180→   180→| Type | Use For | Autonomy |
   181→   181→|------|---------|----------|
   182→   182→| `auto` | Everything Claude can do independently | Fully autonomous |
   183→   183→| `checkpoint:human-verify` | Visual/functional verification | Pauses for user |
   184→   184→| `checkpoint:decision` | Implementation choices | Pauses for user |
   185→   185→| `checkpoint:human-action` | Truly unavoidable manual steps (rare) | Pauses for user |
   186→   186→
   187→   187→**Automation-first rule:** If Claude CAN do it via CLI/API, Claude MUST do it. Checkpoints verify AFTER automation, not replace it.
   188→   188→
   189→   189→## Task Sizing
   190→   190→
   191→   191→Each task: **15-60 minutes** Claude execution time.
   192→   192→
   193→   193→| Duration | Action |
   194→   194→|----------|--------|
   195→   195→| < 15 min | Too small — combine with related task |
   196→   196→| 15-60 min | Right size |
   197→   197→| > 60 min | Too large — split |
   198→   198→
   199→   199→**Too large signals:** Touches >3-5 files, multiple distinct chunks, action section >1 paragraph.
   200→   200→

200→   200→
   201→   201→**Combine signals:** One task sets up for the next, separate tasks touch same file, neither meaningful alone.
   202→   202→
   203→   203→## Interface-First Task Ordering
   204→   204→
   205→   205→When a plan creates new interfaces consumed by subsequent tasks:
   206→   206→
   207→   207→1. **First task: Define contracts** — Create type files, interfaces, exports
   208→   208→2. **Middle tasks: Implement** — Build against the defined contracts
   209→   209→3. **Last task: Wire** — Connect implementations to consumers
   210→   210→
   211→   211→This prevents the "scavenger hunt" anti-pattern where executors explore the codebase to understand contracts. They receive the contracts in the plan itself.
   212→   212→
   213→   213→## Specificity Examples
   214→   214→
   215→   215→| TOO VAGUE | JUST RIGHT |
   216→   216→|-----------|------------|
   217→   217→| "Add authentication" | "Add JWT auth with refresh rotation using jose library, store in httpOnly cookie, 15min access / 7day refresh" |
   218→   218→| "Create the API" | "Create POST /api/projects endpoint accepting {name, description}, validates name length 3-50 chars, returns 201 with project object" |
   219→   219→| "Style the dashboard" | "Add Tailwind classes to Dashboard.tsx: grid layout (3 cols on lg, 1 on mobile), card shadows, hover states on action buttons" |
   220→   220→| "Handle errors" | "Wrap API calls in try/catch, return {error: string} on 4xx/5xx, show toast via sonner on client" |
   221→   221→| "Set up the database" | "Add User and Project models to schema.prisma with UUID ids, email unique constraint, createdAt/updatedAt timestamps, run prisma db push" |
   222→   222→
   223→   223→**Test:** Could a different Claude instance execute without asking clarifying questions? If not, add specificity.
   224→   224→
   225→   225→## TDD Detection
   226→   226→
   227→   227→**Heuristic:** Can you write `expect(fn(input)).toBe(output)` before writing `fn`?
   228→   228→- Yes → Create a dedicated TDD plan (type: tdd)
   229→   229→- No → Standard task in standard plan
   230→   230→
   231→   231→**TDD candidates (dedicated TDD plans):** Business logic with defined I/O, API endpoints with request/response contracts, data transformations, validation rules, algorithms, state machines.
   232→   232→
   233→   233→**Standard tasks:** UI layout/styling, configuration, glue code, one-off scripts, simple CRUD with no business logic.
   234→   234→
   235→   235→**Why TDD gets own plan:** TDD requires RED→GREEN→REFACTOR cycles consuming 40-50% context. Embedding in multi-task plans degrades quality.
   236→   236→
   237→   237→**Task-level TDD** (for code-producing tasks in standard plans): When a task creates or modifies production code, add `tdd="true"` and a `<behavior>` block to make test expectations explicit before implementation:
   238→   238→
   239→   239→```xml
   240→   240→<task type="auto" tdd="true">
   241→   241→  <name>Task: [name]</name>
   242→   242→  <files>src/feature.ts, src/feature.test.ts</files>
   243→   243→  <behavior>
   244→   244→    - Test 1: [expected behavior]
   245→   245→    - Test 2: [edge case]
   246→   246→  </behavior>
   247→   247→  <action>[Implementation after tests pass]</action>
   248→   248→  <verify>
   249→   249→    <automated>npm test -- --filter=feature</automated>
   250→   250→  </verify>
   251→   251→  <done>[Criteria]</done>
   252→   252→</task>
   253→   253→```
   254→   254→
   255→   255→Exceptions where `tdd="true"` is not needed: `type="checkpoint:*"` tasks, configuration-only files, documentation, migration scripts, glue code wiring existing tested components, styling-only changes.
   256→   256→
   257→   257→## User Setup Detection
   258→   258→
   259→   259→For tasks involving external services, identify human-required configuration:
   260→   260→
   261→   261→External service indicators: New SDK (`stripe`, `@sendgrid/mail`, `twilio`, `openai`), webhook handlers, OAuth integration, `process.env.SERVICE_*` patterns.
   262→   262→
   263→   263→For each external service, determine:
   264→   264→1. **Env vars needed** — What secrets from dashboards?
   265→   265→2. **Account setup** — Does user need to create an account?
   266→   266→3. **Dashboard config** — What must be configured in external UI?
   267→   267→
   268→   268→Record in `user_setup` frontmatter. Only include what Claude literally cannot do. Do NOT surface in planning output — execute-plan handles presentation.
   269→   269→
   270→   270→</task_breakdown>
   271→   271→
   272→   272→<dependency_graph>
   273→   273→
   274→   274→## Building the Dependency Graph
   275→   275→
   276→   276→**For each task, record:**
   277→   277→- `needs`: What must exist before this runs
   278→   278→- `creates`: What this produces
   279→   279→- `has_checkpoint`: Requires user interaction?
   280→   280→
   281→   281→**Example with 6 tasks:**
   282→   282→
   283→   283→```
   284→   284→Task A (User model): needs nothing, creates src/models/user.ts
   285→   285→Task B (Product model): needs nothing, creates src/models/product.ts
   286→   286→Task C (User API): needs Task A, creates src/api/users.ts
   287→   287→Task D (Product API): needs Task B, creates src/api/products.ts
   288→   288→Task E (Dashboard): needs Task C + D, creates src/components/Dashboard.tsx
   289→   289→Task F (Verify UI): checkpoint:human-verify, needs Task E
   290→   290→
   291→   291→Graph:
   292→   292→  A --> C --\
   293→   293→              --> E --> F
   294→   294→  B --> D --/
   295→   295→
   296→   296→Wave analysis:
   297→   297→  Wave 1: A, B (independent roots)
   298→   298→  Wave 2: C, D (depend only on Wave 1)
   299→   299→  Wave 3: E (depends on Wave 2)
   300→   300→  Wave 4: F (checkpoint, depends on Wave 3)
   301→   301→```
   302→   302→
   303→   303→## Vertical Slices vs Horizontal Layers
   304→   304→
   305→   305→**Vertical slices (PREFER):**
   306→   306→```
   307→   307→Plan 01: User feature (model + API + UI)
   308→   308→Plan 02: Product feature (model + API + UI)
   309→   309→Plan 03: Order feature (model + API + UI)
   310→   310→```
   311→   311→Result: All three run parallel (Wave 1)
   312→   312→
   313→   313→**Horizontal layers (AVOID):**
   314→   314→```
   315→   315→Plan 01: Create User model, Product model, Order model
   316→   316→Plan 02: Create User API, Product API, Order API
   317→   317→Plan 03: Create User UI, Product UI, Order UI
   318→   318→```
   319→   319→Result: Fully sequential (02 needs 01, 03 needs 02)
   320→   320→
   321→   321→**When vertical slices work:** Features are independent, self-contained, no cross-feature dependencies.
   322→   322→
   323→   323→**When horizontal layers necessary:** Shared foundation required (auth before protected features), genuine type dependencies, infrastructure setup.
   324→   324→
   325→   325→## File Ownership for Parallel Execution
   326→   326→
   327→   327→Exclusive file ownership prevents conflicts:
   328→   328→
   329→   329→```yaml
   330→   330→# Plan 01 frontmatter
   331→   331→files_modified: [src/models/user.ts, src/api/users.ts]
   332→   332→
   333→   333→# Plan 02 frontmatter (no overlap = parallel)
   334→   334→files_modified: [src/models/product.ts, src/api/products.ts]
   335→   335→```
   336→   336→
   337→   337→No overlap → can run parallel. File in multiple plans → later plan depends on earlier.
   338→   338→
   339→   339→</dependency_graph>
   340→   340→
   341→   341→<scope_estimation>
   342→   342→
   343→   343→## Context Budget Rules
   344→   344→
   345→   345→Plans should complete within ~50% context (not 80%). No context anxiety, quality maintained start to finish, room for unexpected complexity.
   346→   346→
   347→   347→**Each plan: 2-3 tasks maximum.**
   348→   348→
   349→   349→| Task Complexity | Tasks/Plan | Context/Task | Total |
   350→   350→|-----------------|------------|--------------|-------|
   351→   351→| Simple (CRUD, config) | 3 | ~10-15% | ~30-45% |
   352→   352→| Complex (auth, payments) | 2 | ~20-30% | ~40-50% |
   353→   353→| Very complex (migrations) | 1-2 | ~30-40% | ~30-50% |
   354→   354→
   355→   355→## Split Signals
   356→   356→
   357→   357→**ALWAYS split if:**
   358→   358→- More than 3 tasks
   359→   359→- Multiple subsystems (DB + API + UI = separate plans)
   360→   360→- Any task with >5 file modifications
   361→   361→- Checkpoint + implementation in same plan
   362→   362→- Discovery + implementation in same plan
   363→   363→
   364→   364→**CONSIDER splitting:** >5 files total, complex domains, uncertainty about approach, natural semantic boundaries.
   365→   365→
   366→   366→## Depth Calibration
   367→   367→
   368→   368→| Depth | Typical Plans/Phase | Tasks/Plan |
   369→   369→|-------|---------------------|------------|
   370→   370→| Quick | 1-3 | 2-3 |
   371→   371→| Standard | 3-5 | 2-3 |
   372→   372→| Comprehensive | 5-10 | 2-3 |
   373→   373→
   374→   374→Derive plans from actual work. Depth determines compression tolerance, not a target. Don't pad small work to hit a number. Don't compress complex work to look efficient.
   375→   375→
   376→   376→## Context Per Task Estimates
   377→   377→
   378→   378→| Files Modified | Context Impact |
   379→   379→|----------------|----------------|
   380→   380→| 0-3 files | ~10-15% (small) |
   381→   381→| 4-6 files | ~20-30% (medium) |
   382→   382→| 7+ files | ~40%+ (split) |
   383→   383→
   384→   384→| Complexity | Context/Task |
   385→   385→|------------|--------------|
   386→   386→| Simple CRUD | ~15% |
   387→   387→| Business logic | ~25% |
   388→   388→| Complex algorithms | ~40% |
   389→   389→| Domain modeling | ~35% |
   390→   390→
   391→   391→</scope_estimation>
   392→   392→
   393→   393→<plan_format>
   394→   394→
   395→   395→## PLAN.md Structure
   396→   396→
   397→   397→```markdown
   398→   398→---
   399→   399→phase: XX-name
   400→   400→plan: NN
   401→   401→type: execute
   402→   402→wave: N                     # Execution wave (1, 2, 3...)
   403→   403→depends_on: []              # Plan IDs this plan requires
   404→   404→files_modified: []          # Files this plan touches
   405→   405→autonomous: true            # false if plan has checkpoints
   406→   406→requirements: []            # REQUIRED — Requirement IDs from ROADMAP this plan addresses. MUST NOT be empty.
   407→   407→user_setup: []              # Human-required setup (omit if empty)
   408→   408→
   409→   409→must_haves:
