1→# Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure - Research
     2→
     3→**Researched:** 2026-03-02
     4→**Domain:** GitHub Actions CI/CD, Kustomize image tag automation, ArgoCD GitOps sync
     5→**Confidence:** HIGH
     6→
     7→## Summary
     8→
     9→Phase 27 closes the GitOps loop: a push to `master` triggers the existing `container-images.yml` workflow which builds and pushes SHA-tagged images to ghcr.io, then a new job in that same workflow updates the Kustomize dev overlay `kustomization.yaml` files with the new SHA-based image tags and commits the change back to the repo. ArgoCD (installed in Phase 26 with automated sync + self-heal) detects the Git change and rolls out updated pods automatically.
    10→
    11→The existing infrastructure is well-positioned for this. The `container-images.yml` workflow already builds all three images (apiservice, gateway, web) with `type=sha,prefix=sha-` tags via `docker/metadata-action@v5`, producing tags like `sha-860c190`. The Kustomize dev overlays (`infra/k8s/overlays/dev/{service}/kustomization.yaml`) already have per-service `images:` blocks with `newName` and `newTag` fields. ArgoCD Applications already point to these overlay paths with automated sync enabled. The only missing piece is a GitHub Actions job that updates `newTag` in each overlay's `kustomization.yaml` and commits the change.
    12→
    13→A critical design consideration is infinite loop prevention. When the workflow commits updated image tags back to `master`, it must not re-trigger itself. GitHub Actions has built-in protection: events triggered by `GITHUB_TOKEN` do **not** create new workflow runs (except for `workflow_dispatch` and `repository_dispatch`). Since the `container-images.yml` workflow triggers on `push: branches: [master]`, a commit pushed via `GITHUB_TOKEN` will not re-trigger it. As an additional safety measure, the commit message should include `[skip ci]`.
    14→
    15→**Primary recommendation:** Add an `update-manifests` job to the existing `container-images.yml` workflow that runs after all three build jobs complete. This job checks out the repo, runs `kustomize edit set image` in each per-service overlay directory to update `newTag` to `sha-<short-sha>`, commits the three modified `kustomization.yaml` files, and pushes to `master` using `GITHUB_TOKEN` with `contents: write` permission. The overlay `newName` fields must also change from local names (`apiservice`, `gateway`, `web`) to the full ghcr.io registry paths.
    16→
    17→<phase_requirements>
    18→## Phase Requirements
    19→
    20→| ID | Description | Research Support |
    21→|----|-------------|-----------------|
    22→| CICD-02 | CI commits updated SHA image tags to Kustomize dev overlay, triggering ArgoCD sync | The `update-manifests` job uses `kustomize edit set image` to write `sha-<short-sha>` tags into each per-service overlay kustomization.yaml, commits with `[skip ci]`, and pushes via GITHUB_TOKEN. ArgoCD auto-sync detects the tag change within its 3-minute polling interval (or immediately with a webhook) and rolls out new pods. Every image tag in Git matches the commit SHA that built it, providing full traceability. |
    23→</phase_requirements>
    24→
    25→## Standard Stack
    26→
    27→### Core
    28→| Tool | Version | Purpose | Why Standard |
    29→|------|---------|---------|--------------|
    30→| GitHub Actions | N/A (platform) | CI/CD pipeline; build images + commit manifest updates | Already in use for `container-images.yml`; no new tooling needed |
    31→| Kustomize | built-in (kubectl) | `kustomize edit set image` updates overlay `kustomization.yaml` | Already used in overlays; CLI command is the standard automation pattern for image tag updates |
    32→| docker/metadata-action | v5 | Generates SHA-prefixed image tags (`sha-860c190`) | Already in use in all three build jobs; provides consistent tag format |
    33→| ArgoCD | v3.3.2 | Detects Git changes, syncs cluster state automatically | Installed in Phase 26; auto-sync + self-heal already enabled on all Applications |
    34→
    35→### Supporting
    36→| Tool | Version | Purpose | When to Use |
    37→|------|---------|---------|-------------|
    38→| actions/checkout@v4 | v4 | Checkout repo in the update-manifests job | Required to access and modify kustomization.yaml files |
    39→| git (CLI) | built-in | Commit and push updated kustomization.yaml files | Standard approach; no third-party action needed for simple commit+push |
    40→
    41→### Alternatives Considered
    42→| Instead of | Could Use | Tradeoff |
    43→|------------|-----------|----------|
    44→| `kustomize edit set image` CLI | Direct sed/yq replacement of newTag | `kustomize edit set image` is the official Kustomize CLI command; sed is fragile and breaks on format changes |
    45→| Commit in same workflow | Separate `repository_dispatch` event | Adds complexity; same-workflow job is simpler and runs only when builds succeed |
    46→| `stefanzweifel/git-auto-commit-action` | Manual `git commit && git push` | Third-party action adds a dependency; manual git commands are ~5 lines and fully transparent |
    47→| ArgoCD polling (3 min default) | GitHub webhook to ArgoCD `/api/webhook` | Webhook gives <5s detection but requires ArgoCD to be internet-accessible; kind cluster is local-only. Polling is sufficient for the success criterion (5 min). Can add webhook later for cloud deployments. |
    48→| Short SHA tag (`sha-860c190`) | Full commit SHA tag | Short SHA (7 chars) is the `docker/metadata-action` default with `type=sha,prefix=sha-`; sufficient for uniqueness and traceability. Already used in the existing workflow. |
    49→
    50→**Installation:**
    51→```bash
    52→# No new tools to install. Kustomize is built into the GitHub Actions runner via kubectl.
    53→# If standalone kustomize is needed:
    54→# - uses: imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2
    55→# But kubectl built-in kustomize is sufficient.
    56→```
    57→
    58→## Architecture Patterns
    59→
    60→### Recommended Workflow Structure
    61→```
    62→.github/workflows/container-images.yml
    63→  build-web:         (existing) Build + push web image to ghcr.io
    64→  build-apiservice:  (existing) Build + push apiservice image to ghcr.io
    65→  build-gateway:     (existing) Build + push gateway image to ghcr.io
    66→  update-manifests:  (NEW) Runs after all 3 builds complete
    67→    - Checkout repo
    68→    - kustomize edit set image for each overlay
    69→    - git commit + push [skip ci]
    70→```
    71→
    72→### Pattern 1: Update-Manifests Job with `needs` Dependency
    73→**What:** A GitHub Actions job that depends on all three build jobs (`needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]`), updates Kustomize overlays with the new SHA tag, and commits the change.
    74→**When to use:** When all images in a monorepo are built and deployed together.
    75→**Example:**
    76→```yaml
    77→# Source: Standard GitOps pattern verified via official Kustomize docs + GitHub Actions docs
    78→update-manifests:
    79→  name: Update Kustomize Manifests
    80→  needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]
    81→  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    82→  permissions:
    83→    contents: write
    84→  steps:
    85→    - name: Checkout
    86→      uses: actions/checkout@v4
    87→
    88→    - name: Update image tags in dev overlay
    89→      env:
    90→        SHORT_SHA: sha-${{ github.sha }}  # Will be trimmed to 7 chars below
    91→      run: |
    92→        SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
    93→
    94→        cd infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice
    95→        kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:$SHORT_SHA
    96→
    97→        cd ../gateway
    98→        kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/gateway:$SHORT_SHA
    99→
   100→        cd ../web
   101→        kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/web:$SHORT_SHA
   102→
   103→    - name: Commit and push
   104→      run: |
   105→        git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
   106→        git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
   107→        git add infra/k8s/overlays/dev/
   108→        git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes to commit" && exit 0
   109→        git commit -m "[skip ci] chore: update image tags to sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   110→        git push
   111→```
   112→
   113→### Pattern 2: Overlay Image Name Correction
   114→**What:** The current dev overlay kustomization.yaml files use local-only image names (`apiservice:dev`, `gateway:dev`, `web:dev`) that match the bootstrap script's local builds. For CI/CD, the `newName` must point to the ghcr.io registry path.
   115→**When to use:** When the same overlay serves both local development (kind with local images) and CI/CD (ghcr.io images).
   116→**Key insight:** `kustomize edit set image` with the syntax `<oldName>=<newName>:<newTag>` updates both `newName` and `newTag` in one command. Running `kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190` updates the overlay from:
   117→```yaml
   118→images:
   119→- name: apiservice
   120→  newName: apiservice
   121→  newTag: dev
   122→```
   123→to:
   124→```yaml
   125→images:
   126→- name: apiservice
   127→  newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice
   128→  newTag: sha-860c190
   129→```
   130→This means the base deployment's `image: apiservice:dev` gets transformed by Kustomize to `image: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190`.
   131→
   132→### Pattern 3: SHA Tag Format Alignment
   133→**What:** The `docker/metadata-action` `type=sha,prefix=sha-` generates tags like `sha-860c190` (7-character short SHA). The `update-manifests` job must produce the same format.
   134→**When to use:** Always -- the tag in `kustomization.yaml` must exactly match a tag that exists in ghcr.io.
   135→**Example:**
   136→```bash
   137→# docker/metadata-action produces: sha-860c190 (7 chars)
   138→# To match in shell:
   139→SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   140→# Result: sha-860c190
   141→```
   142→
   143→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   144→- **Using `latest` tag in kustomization.yaml:** Defeats traceability. Every deployment must map to a specific commit SHA.
   145→- **Committing with a PAT instead of GITHUB_TOKEN:** PAT-based commits trigger subsequent workflows, risking infinite loops. GITHUB_TOKEN commits do not trigger push-based workflows.
   146→- **Running `kustomize edit set image` in the base directory:** The base deployment.yaml has the original image reference; the overlay is where environment-specific image overrides belong.
   147→- **Hardcoding the SHA in the workflow:** Use `${{ github.sha }}` and `cut -c1-7` to derive the short SHA dynamically.
   148→- **Skipping the `git diff --staged --quiet` check:** If no files changed (e.g., re-run of the same commit), the commit step should be a no-op rather than failing.
   149→- **Modifying overlay for tag-only push (v\*.\*.\*):** The workflow triggers on both `push: branches: [master]` and `push: tags: ['v*.*.*']`. The manifest update should only happen for branch pushes to `master`, not for tag pushes (which are release artifacts, not deployments).
   150→
   151→## Don't Hand-Roll
   152→
   153→| Problem | Don't Build | Use Instead | Why |
   154→|---------|-------------|-------------|-----|
   155→| Image tag update in YAML | Custom sed/awk to replace `newTag:` | `kustomize edit set image` | Handles YAML formatting, multi-line values, comments; official CLI command |
   156→| Git commit automation | Custom shell script with error handling | `git config && git add && git commit && git push` in workflow step | Simple enough for 5 lines; no third-party action needed |
   157→| SHA tag generation | Custom hashing or tag derivation | `docker/metadata-action` `type=sha,prefix=sha-` | Already generates correct format; just derive matching format in update job |
   158→| ArgoCD sync trigger | Custom API call to ArgoCD | ArgoCD's built-in Git polling (3 min) | Polling is sufficient for 5-minute SLA; webhook requires internet-accessible ArgoCD |
   159→
   160→**Key insight:** The entire GitOps loop closure is ~30 lines of workflow YAML added to an existing file. No new tools, no new repos, no complex infrastructure. The hard work was done in Phases 23-26.
   161→
   162→## Common Pitfalls
   163→
   164→### Pitfall 1: Infinite Workflow Loop
   165→**What goes wrong:** The `update-manifests` job commits to `master`, which triggers `container-images.yml` again, which builds images and commits again, forever.
   166→**Why it happens:** If using a PAT or misconfigured token, push events can re-trigger the same workflow.
   167→**How to avoid:** Use `GITHUB_TOKEN` (not a PAT) for the git push. GitHub Actions does not trigger `push`-based workflows for commits made by `GITHUB_TOKEN`. As a belt-and-suspenders measure, include `[skip ci]` in the commit message.
   168→**Warning signs:** Multiple identical workflow runs in quick succession; the Actions tab shows a cascade of runs.
   169→
   170→### Pitfall 2: SHA Tag Mismatch
   171→**What goes wrong:** The tag in `kustomization.yaml` (e.g., `sha-860c190`) does not match any tag pushed to ghcr.io, causing `ImagePullBackOff` in the cluster.
   172→**Why it happens:** The `docker/metadata-action` uses a 7-character short SHA by default, but the update-manifests job uses a different length or format.
   173→**How to avoid:** Use `echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7` to produce the same 7-character short SHA. Verify by checking `docker/metadata-action` step output in a previous run.
   174→**Warning signs:** Pods in `ImagePullBackOff`; `kubectl describe pod` shows "manifest unknown" or "not found" for the image tag.
   175→
   176→### Pitfall 3: Race Condition on Push
   177→**What goes wrong:** The `update-manifests` job checks out the repo at the triggering commit, but another commit lands on `master` before the push, causing a `push rejected (non-fast-forward)` error.
   178→**Why it happens:** In a high-traffic repo, multiple pushes to `master` can overlap.
   179→**How to avoid:** For this project (single developer), this is extremely unlikely. If it becomes an issue, add `git pull --rebase` before `git push`, or use a retry step. The workflow will simply fail and the next push will succeed.
   180→**Warning signs:** `update-manifests` job fails with "non-fast-forward" error.
   181→
   182→### Pitfall 4: Tag Push Triggers Manifest Update
   183→**What goes wrong:** A release tag push (`v1.0.0`) triggers the workflow, and the `update-manifests` job updates overlays with the SHA of the tag commit, overwriting the latest `master` SHA tag.
   184→**Why it happens:** The workflow triggers on both `push: branches: [master]` and `push: tags: ['v*.*.*']`.
   185→**How to avoid:** Add an `if` condition to the `update-manifests` job: `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'`. This ensures manifest updates only happen for branch pushes, not tag pushes.
   186→**Warning signs:** Overlay kustomization.yaml being updated on tag pushes with non-master SHAs.
   187→
   188→### Pitfall 5: Overlay `newName` Still Points to Local Image
   189→**What goes wrong:** The overlay has `newName: apiservice` (local Docker name) instead of `newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice` (registry path). ArgoCD syncs the manifest, but the pod tries to pull `apiservice:sha-860c190` from Docker Hub instead of ghcr.io.
   190→**Why it happens:** The current overlays were set up for local kind development where images are loaded directly. CI/CD images live in ghcr.io.
   191→**How to avoid:** `kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-xxx` updates both `newName` and `newTag` simultaneously. This is the correct command to use.
   192→**Warning signs:** Pods in `ImagePullBackOff` with "repository does not exist" error.
   193→
   194→### Pitfall 6: Bootstrap Script Conflicts with CI/CD Overlays
   195→**What goes wrong:** The bootstrap script builds local images tagged `dev` and the overlays reference `newTag: dev`. After CI/CD updates the overlay to `sha-xxx`, the bootstrap script no longer works because the local kind cluster doesn't have `ghcr.io/...` images.
   196→**Why it happens:** The overlay serves two purposes: local development and CI/CD deployment.
   197→**How to avoid:** Accept that the overlay in Git reflects the last CI/CD deployment. For local development, the bootstrap script can either: (a) run `kustomize edit set image` to reset overlays to local tags after applying, or (b) be updated to also tag local images with the same format. The simplest approach is to accept that the overlay reflects CI/CD state and the bootstrap script's local image loading works independently (ArgoCD is not used in local kind development without the full CI/CD pipeline).
   198→**Warning signs:** Bootstrap script succeeds but ArgoCD shows OutOfSync because overlay tags don't match loaded images.
   199→
   200→## Code Examples
   201→
   202→Verified patterns from official sources:
   203→
   204→### Complete `update-manifests` Job
   205→```yaml
   206→# Source: Standard GitOps pattern from Kustomize + GitHub Actions docs
   207→update-manifests:
   208→  name: Update Kustomize Manifests
   209→  needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]
   210→  if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'
   211→  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   212→  permissions:
   213→    contents: write
   214→  steps:
   215→    - name: Checkout
   216→      uses: actions/checkout@v4
   217→
   218→    - name: Update image tags in dev overlay
   219→      run: |
   220→        SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   221→        echo "Updating image tags to: $SHORT_SHA"
   222→
   223→        cd infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice
   224→        kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:$SHORT_SHA
   225→
   226→        cd ../gateway
   227→        kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/gateway:$SHORT_SHA
   228→
   229→        cd ../web
   230→        kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/web:$SHORT_SHA
   231→
   232→    - name: Commit and push
   233→      run: |
   234→        git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
   235→        git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
   236→        git add infra/k8s/overlays/dev/
   237→        git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes to commit" && exit 0
   238→        git commit -m "[skip ci] chore: update image tags to sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   239→        git push
   240→```
   241→
   242→### Kustomize Edit Set Image Command
   243→```bash
   244→# Source: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/blob/master/examples/image.md
   245→# Syntax: kustomize edit set image <oldName>=<newRegistry>/<newName>:<newTag>
   246→
   247→# Before (in kustomization.yaml):
   248→# images:
   249→# - name: apiservice
   250→#   newName: apiservice
   251→#   newTag: dev
   252→
   253→kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190
   254→
   255→# After (in kustomization.yaml):
   256→# images:
   257→# - name: apiservice
   258→#   newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice
   259→#   newTag: sha-860c190
   260→```
   261→
   262→### SHA Tag Derivation
   263→```bash
   264→# docker/metadata-action with type=sha,prefix=sha- produces: sha-860c190 (7 chars)
   265→# To match in shell:
   266→SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   267→# For full SHA: ${{ github.sha }} = 860c1904a1ce19322e91ac35af1ab07466440c37
   268→# Short: sha-860c190
   269→```
   270→
   271→### Verifying the Loop End-to-End
   272→```bash
   273→# 1. Check the latest image tag in the overlay
   274→cat infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice/kustomization.yaml
   275→# images:
   276→# - name: apiservice
   277→#   newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice
   278→#   newTag: sha-860c190
   279→
   280→# 2. Check ArgoCD sees the updated Application
   281→kubectl get applications -n argocd -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.status.sync.status}{"\n"}{end}'
   282→
   283→# 3. Check the running pod's image matches
   284→kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.spec.containers[0].image}{"\n"}{end}'
   285→# apiservice-xxx   ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:sha-860c190
   286→```
   287→
   288→## State of the Art
   289→
   290→| Old Approach | Current Approach | When Changed | Impact |
   291→|--------------|------------------|--------------|--------|
   292→| Separate config repo for manifests | Single repo (app + infra in same repo) | Ongoing pattern | Simpler for single-team projects; `GITHUB_TOKEN` can commit to same repo |
   293→| Custom webhook handlers for ArgoCD | ArgoCD built-in 3-min polling or `/api/webhook` | ArgoCD 1.x+ | No custom infrastructure needed for GitOps sync trigger |
   294→| `sed -i` to replace image tags | `kustomize edit set image` CLI | Kustomize 3.x+ | YAML-aware, handles formatting, official tool |
   295→| PAT-based commits in CI | GITHUB_TOKEN with `contents: write` | GitHub Actions 2021+ | Built-in infinite loop prevention; no token management overhead |
   296→
   297→**Deprecated/outdated:**
   298→- Using `sed` to modify Kustomize files: fragile, not YAML-aware
   299→- Separate GitOps repository for manifests: adds complexity for single-team projects; single-repo is standard for small projects
   300→- ArgoCD Image Updater (write-back): designed for auto-updating to latest tags; not needed when CI explicitly sets the tag
   301→
   302→## Open Questions
   303→
   304→1. **Bootstrap script compatibility after CI/CD overlay update**
   305→   - What we know: The bootstrap script builds local images tagged `dev` and uses overlays with `newTag: dev`. After CI/CD, the overlay will have `newTag: sha-xxx` pointing to ghcr.io.
   306→   - What's unclear: Should the bootstrap script be updated to handle this divergence, or is it acceptable that the overlay in Git reflects CI/CD state?
   307→   - Recommendation: Accept the divergence. The bootstrap script's Step 10-11 (build/load images) and Step 12 (apply ArgoCD root app) work independently. For local development, the user can either: (a) let the bootstrap script work as-is (ArgoCD will sync from Git, pulling ghcr.io images if the cluster has internet), or (b) after bootstrap, manually reset overlays with `kustomize edit set image apiservice=apiservice:dev` etc. This is a local dev workflow concern, not a CI/CD concern. Defer to implementation.
   308→
   309→2. **ArgoCD sync latency vs 5-minute SLA**
   310→   - What we know: ArgoCD polls Git every 3 minutes by default. The success criterion requires a new pod within 5 minutes of CI workflow completing.
   311→   - What's unclear: Whether 3-minute polling + pod rollout time fits within 5 minutes.
   312→   - Recommendation: 3-minute polling + ~30s for pod rollout = ~3.5 minutes typical, well within the 5-minute SLA. If tighter latency is needed later, configure a GitHub webhook to ArgoCD's `/api/webhook` endpoint. Not needed for this phase.
   313→
   314→3. **`contents: write` permission scope**
   315→   - What we know: The `update-manifests` job needs `permissions: contents: write` to push commits. The build jobs currently have `permissions: contents: read, packages: write`.
   316→   - What's unclear: Whether job-level permissions can differ from workflow-level permissions.
   317→   - Recommendation: GitHub Actions supports per-job permissions. Set `contents: write` only on the `update-manifests` job. The build jobs keep their existing `contents: read` + `packages: write`.
   318→
   319→## Validation Architecture
   320→
   321→### Test Framework
   322→| Property | Value |
   323→|----------|-------|
   324→| Framework | Manual validation via GitHub Actions run + kubectl (infrastructure/CI phase, no unit test framework) |
   325→| Config file | `.github/workflows/container-images.yml` |
   326→| Quick run command | Check workflow run status in GitHub Actions tab |
   327→| Full suite command | Push to master, verify: (1) workflow completes, (2) overlay kustomization.yaml updated in Git, (3) ArgoCD syncs, (4) pods running new image |
   328→
   329→### Phase Requirements to Test Map
   330→| Req ID | Behavior | Test Type | Automated Command | File Exists? |
   331→|--------|----------|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
   332→| CICD-02 | CI commits updated SHA image tags to Kustomize dev overlay | smoke | `git log --oneline -1 -- infra/k8s/overlays/dev/` (verify [skip ci] commit) | N/A -- Git history |
   333→| CICD-02 | Image tag in overlay matches commit SHA | smoke | `grep newTag infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice/kustomization.yaml` (verify sha-XXX format) | N/A -- file content |
   334→| CICD-02 | ArgoCD syncs updated manifests | smoke | `kubectl get applications -n argocd -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.sync.status}'` | N/A -- kubectl |
   335→| CICD-02 | Pod runs image matching commit SHA | smoke | `kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[0].image}'` | N/A -- kubectl |
   336→| CICD-02 | Workflow does not trigger infinite loop | smoke | Check GitHub Actions tab shows no cascading runs after the [skip ci] commit | N/A -- GitHub UI |
   337→
   338→### Sampling Rate
   339→- **Per task commit:** Verify workflow YAML syntax with `act --dryrun` or visual review
   340→- **Per wave merge:** Push to master, verify full loop: build -> commit -> ArgoCD sync -> pod update
   341→- **Phase gate:** Both success criteria verified before `/gsd:verify-work`
   342→
   343→### Wave 0 Gaps
   344→None -- this phase modifies a GitHub Actions workflow file and Kustomize overlays. No test framework infrastructure needed.
   345→
   346→## Sources
   347→
   348→### Primary (HIGH confidence)
   349→- [/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kustomize/blob/master/examples/image.md) - `kustomize edit set image` command syntax and behavior verified via Context7
   350→- [docker/metadata-action@v5](https://github.com/docker/metadata-action) - SHA tag format: `type=sha,prefix=sha-` produces `sha-<7chars>` by default
   351→- [GitHub Actions: Skipping workflow runs](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/managing-workflow-runs-and-deployments/managing-workflow-runs/skipping-workflow-runs) - `[skip ci]` in commit message skips push/PR-triggered workflows
   352→- [GitHub Actions: GITHUB_TOKEN](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/25702) - Events triggered by GITHUB_TOKEN do not create new workflow runs (except workflow_dispatch/repository_dispatch)
   353→- [ArgoCD Git Webhook Configuration](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/latest/operator-manual/webhook/) - Webhook endpoint at `/api/webhook` for immediate sync (optional, not needed for this phase)
   354→- [ArgoCD Auto Sync](https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user-guide/auto_sync/) - Default 3-minute polling interval; selfHeal and automated sync configuration
   355→
   356→### Secondary (MEDIUM confidence)
   357→- [GitOps CI/CD patterns with GitHub Actions + ArgoCD](https://medium.com/@mehmetkanus17/argocd-github-actions-a-complete-gitops-ci-cd-workflow-for-kubernetes-applications-ed2f91d37641) - Workflow structure with `needs:` dependency for update-manifest job
   358→- [Automation with GitHub Actions and Kustomize](https://gap.gjensidige.io/docs/guides/ci-automation-with-kustomize) - `kustomize edit set image` in CI/CD pipelines pattern
   359→
   360→### Tertiary (LOW confidence)
   361→- None -- all findings verified with official documentation
   362→
   363→## Metadata
   364→
   365→**Confidence breakdown:**
   366→- Standard stack: HIGH - No new tools needed; existing `container-images.yml`, Kustomize, and ArgoCD are all already in place. Only addition is a new job in an existing workflow.
   367→- Architecture: HIGH - The `update-manifests` job pattern is the standard GitOps CI/CD pattern, verified across multiple official and community sources. `kustomize edit set image` syntax verified via Context7.
   368→- Pitfalls: HIGH - Infinite loop prevention via GITHUB_TOKEN is documented by GitHub. SHA format alignment is straightforward. Race condition is theoretical for single-developer repo.
   369→
   370→**Research date:** 2026-03-02
   371→**Valid until:** 2026-04-02 (30 days -- GitHub Actions and Kustomize are stable; ArgoCD sync behavior is well-established)
   372→

   121→  <name>Task 1: Add update-manifests job to container-images.yml</name>
   122→  <files>.github/workflows/container-images.yml</files>
   123→  <action>
   124→    Append a new `update-manifests` job to the existing `.github/workflows/container-images.yml` workflow file. Add it after the `build-gateway` job (at the end of the jobs section).
   125→
   126→    The new job definition:
   127→    ```yaml
   128→    update-manifests:
   129→      name: Update Kustomize Manifests
   130→      needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]
   131→      if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'
   132→      runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   133→      permissions:
   134→        contents: write
   135→      steps:
   136→        - name: Checkout
   137→          uses: actions/checkout@v4
   138→
   139→        - name: Update image tags in dev overlay
   140→          run: |
   141→            SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   142→            echo "Updating image tags to: $SHORT_SHA"
   143→
   144→            cd infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice
   145→            kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:$SHORT_SHA
   146→
   147→            cd ../gateway
   148→            kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/gateway:$SHORT_SHA
   149→
   150→            cd ../web
   151→            kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/web:$SHORT_SHA
   152→
   153→        - name: Commit and push
   154→          run: |
   155→            git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
   156→            git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
   157→            git add infra/k8s/overlays/dev/
   158→            git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes to commit" && exit 0
   159→            git commit -m "[skip ci] chore: update image tags to sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   160→            git push
   161→    ```
   162→
   163→    Key design decisions:
   164→    1. **`needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]`** -- only runs after ALL 3 images are successfully built and pushed. If any build fails, manifests are not updated.
   165→    2. **`if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'`** -- prevents manifest updates on tag pushes (`v*.*.*`), which are release artifacts, not deployments.
   166→    3. **`permissions: contents: write`** -- job-level permission, scoped only to this job. The existing build jobs keep their `contents: read` + `packages: write`.
   167→    4. **`kustomize edit set image <oldName>=<newName>:<newTag>`** -- updates both `newName` (to ghcr.io registry path) and `newTag` (to SHA) in one command. This is the official Kustomize CLI pattern, not fragile sed.
   168→    5. **Short SHA format: `sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)`** -- matches the exact format produced by `docker/metadata-action`'s `type=sha,prefix=sha-` (7-character short SHA with `sha-` prefix).
   169→    6. **`git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes" && exit 0`** -- prevents empty commits if the workflow is re-run on the same commit (idempotent).
   170→    7. **`[skip ci]` in commit message** -- belt-and-suspenders protection against infinite loops. GITHUB_TOKEN already prevents re-triggering push-based workflows, but `[skip ci]` is an additional safety net.
   171→    8. **No PAT** -- the default GITHUB_TOKEN is used (no explicit token configuration needed). GitHub Actions does not trigger push-based workflows for commits made by GITHUB_TOKEN.
   172→
   173→    DO NOT modify any existing job (build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway). Only append the new job.
   174→  </action>

   177→  </verify>
   178→  <done>

   180→    - Job depends on all 3 build jobs (needs clause)
   181→    - Job only runs on master branch pushes (if condition)
   182→    - Job has contents: write permission
   183→    - kustomize edit set image updates all 3 service overlays with ghcr.io registry path + SHA tag
   184→    - git commit uses [skip ci] message
   185→    - git diff --staged --quiet prevents empty commits
   186→    - SHA format matches docker/metadata-action output (sha- prefix + 7 chars)
   187→    - No existing jobs modified
   188→  </done>

input.old_string:
Key design decisions:
    1. **`needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]`** -- only runs after ALL 3 images are successfully built and pushed. If any build fails, manifests are not updated.
    2. **`if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'`** -- prevents manifest updates on tag pushes (`v*.*.*`), which are release artifacts, not deployments.
    3. **`permissions: contents: write`** -- job-level permission, scoped only to this job. The existing build jobs keep their `contents: read` + `packages: write`.
    4. **`kustomize edit set image <oldName>=<newName>:<newTag>`** -- updates both `newName` (to ghcr.io registry path) and `newTag` (to SHA) in one command. This is the official Kustomize CLI pattern, not fragile sed.
input.new_string:
Key design decisions:
    1. **`needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]`** -- only runs after ALL 3 images are successfully built and pushed. If any build fails, manifests are not updated.
    2. **`if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'`** -- prevents manifest updates on tag pushes (`v*.*.*`), which are release artifacts, not deployments.
    3. **`permissions: contents: write`** -- job-level permission, scoped only to this job. The existing build jobs keep their `contents: read` + `packages: write`.
    4. **`imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2`** -- installs the standalone kustomize CLI binary. The `ubuntu-latest` GitHub Actions runner does NOT ship with standalone `kustomize` (it only ships `kubectl`, and `kubectl kustomize` is read-only -- it does not support `edit` subcommands). This step MUST run before any `kustomize edit set image` command.
    5. **`kustomize edit set image <oldName>=<newName>:<newTag>`** -- updates both `newName` (to ghcr.io registry path) and `newTag` (to SHA) in one command. This is the official Kustomize CLI pattern, not fragile sed.

input.old_string:
5. **Short SHA format: `sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)`** -- matches the exact format produced by `docker/metadata-action`'s `type=sha,prefix=sha-` (7-character short SHA with `sha-` prefix).
    6. **`git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes" && exit 0`** -- prevents empty commits if the workflow is re-run on the same commit (idempotent).
    7. **`[skip ci]` in commit message** -- belt-and-suspenders protection against infinite loops. GITHUB_TOKEN already prevents re-triggering push-based workflows, but `[skip ci]` is an additional safety net.
    8. **No PAT** -- the default GITHUB_TOKEN is used (no explicit token configuration needed). GitHub Actions does not trigger push-based workflows for commits made by GITHUB_TOKEN.
input.new_string:
6. **Short SHA format: `sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)`** -- matches the exact format produced by `docker/metadata-action`'s `type=sha,prefix=sha-` (7-character short SHA with `sha-` prefix).
    7. **`git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes" && exit 0`** -- prevents empty commits if the workflow is re-run on the same commit (idempotent).
    8. **`[skip ci]` in commit message** -- belt-and-suspenders protection against infinite loops. GITHUB_TOKEN already prevents re-triggering push-based workflows, but `[skip ci]` is an additional safety net.
    9. **No PAT** -- the default GITHUB_TOKEN is used (no explicit token configuration needed). GitHub Actions does not trigger push-based workflows for commits made by GITHUB_TOKEN.

content.text:
The file looks correct. All changes are consistent and properly applied. Here is a summary of what changed:

---

## Changes Made to `27-01-PLAN.md`

### Issue 1 (blocker): Missing kustomize CLI installation step

**Problem:** The plan called `kustomize edit set image` but the standalone `kustomize` CLI binary is not pre-installed on `ubuntu-latest` GitHub Actions runners. The runner ships with `kubectl`, but `kubectl kustomize` is read-only and does not support the `edit` subcommand. The job would fail with `kustomize: command not found`.

**Changes made (6 locations):**

1. **`must_haves.truths`** (line 17): Added new truth: `"A Setup Kustomize step using imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2 runs before kustomize edit set image (standalone kustomize CLI is not pre-installed on ubuntu-latest runners)"`

2. **`must_haves.key_links`** (lines 30-33): Added new key_link from the update-manifests job to `kustomize CLI binary on runner` via `imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2`. Updated existing key_links' `via` field to note `(requires setup-kustomize step)`.

3. **Task 1 YAML block** (lines 146-147): Added the `Setup Kustomize` step between `Checkout` and `Update image tags in dev overlay`:
   ```yaml
   - name: Setup Kustomize
     uses: imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2
   ```

4. **Task 1 design decisions** (line 177): Added decision #4 explaining why `imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2` is required. Renumbered subsequent decisions 5-9.

5. **Task 1 verify block** (line 187): Added `grep -q "setup-kustomize@v2"` check.

6. **Task 1 done criteria** (line 194): Added `"Setup Kustomize step (imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2) installs standalone kustomize CLI before edit commands"`.

7. **Task 2 action** (line 217): Added verification that `Setup Kustomize` step exists BEFORE `Update image tags` step.

8. **Overall verification** (line 250): Added check #5: `grep "setup-kustomize@v2"`.

9. **success_criteria** (line 258): Added `"The job installs standalone kustomize CLI via imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2 before running edit commands"`.

---

### Issue 2 (warning): Missing operator-observable truths

**Problem:** All 8 original `must_haves.truths` were implementation details about workflow YAML structure. None captured the operator-observable outcome that overlay files in Git are updated with SHA tags after a CI run, or that no infinite loop occurs. The phase goal ("push to master automatically flows through...without manual intervention") was not reflected in any truth.

**Changes made (2 locations):**

1. **`must_haves.truths`** (lines 23-24): Added two operator-observable truths:
   - `"After a push to master, each infra/k8s/overlays/dev/*/kustomization.yaml in Git shows newTag: sha-<7chars> matching the triggering commit SHA"`
   - `"The [skip ci] commit does not trigger a subsequent container-images.yml workflow run (GITHUB_TOKEN + [skip ci] double protection)"`

2. **`success_criteria`** (lines 265-266): Added two matching operator-observable criteria:
   - `"Operator-observable: after a push to master completes, each infra/k8s/overlays/dev/*/kustomization.yaml in Git shows newTag: sha-<7chars> matching the triggering commit SHA"`
   - `"Operator-observable: the [skip ci] manifest commit does not trigger a subsequent container-images.yml workflow run"`

   108→After the update-manifests job runs, each overlay will look like:
   109→```yaml
   110→images:
   111→- name: {service}
   112→  newName: ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/{service}
   113→  newTag: sha-860c190
   114→```
   115→
   116→<!-- ArgoCD sync behavior (from Phase 26) -->
   117→ArgoCD is configured with:
   118→- Auto-sync enabled on all child Applications
   119→- Self-heal enabled (reverts manual changes)
   120→- 3-minute default polling interval
   121→- Each child Application points to its per-service overlay directory
   122→</interfaces>
   123→</context>

   125→<tasks>
   126→
   127→<task type="auto">
   128→  <name>Task 1: Add update-manifests job to container-images.yml</name>
   129→  <files>.github/workflows/container-images.yml</files>
   130→  <action>
   131→    Append a new `update-manifests` job to the existing `.github/workflows/container-images.yml` workflow file. Add it after the `build-gateway` job (at the end of the jobs section).
   132→
   133→    The new job definition:
   134→    ```yaml
   135→    update-manifests:
   136→      name: Update Kustomize Manifests
   137→      needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]
   138→      if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'
   139→      runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   140→      permissions:
   141→        contents: write
   142→      steps:
   143→        - name: Checkout
   144→          uses: actions/checkout@v4
   145→
   146→        - name: Setup Kustomize
   147→          uses: imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2
   148→
   149→        - name: Update image tags in dev overlay
   150→          run: |
   151→            SHORT_SHA="sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   152→            echo "Updating image tags to: $SHORT_SHA"
   153→
   154→            cd infra/k8s/overlays/dev/apiservice
   155→            kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/apiservice:$SHORT_SHA
   156→
   157→            cd ../gateway
   158→            kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/gateway:$SHORT_SHA
   159→
   160→            cd ../web
   161→            kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io/baotoq/micro-commerce/web:$SHORT_SHA
   162→
   163→        - name: Commit and push
   164→          run: |
   165→            git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
   166→            git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
   167→            git add infra/k8s/overlays/dev/
   168→            git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes to commit" && exit 0
   169→            git commit -m "[skip ci] chore: update image tags to sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)"
   170→            git push
   171→    ```
   172→
   173→    Key design decisions:
   174→    1. **`needs: [build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway]`** -- only runs after ALL 3 images are successfully built and pushed. If any build fails, manifests are not updated.
   175→    2. **`if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'`** -- prevents manifest updates on tag pushes (`v*.*.*`), which are release artifacts, not deployments.
   176→    3. **`permissions: contents: write`** -- job-level permission, scoped only to this job. The existing build jobs keep their `contents: read` + `packages: write`.
   177→    4. **`imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2`** -- installs the standalone kustomize CLI binary. The `ubuntu-latest` GitHub Actions runner does NOT ship with standalone `kustomize` (it only ships `kubectl`, and `kubectl kustomize` is read-only -- it does not support `edit` subcommands). This step MUST run before any `kustomize edit set image` command.
   178→    5. **`kustomize edit set image <oldName>=<newName>:<newTag>`** -- updates both `newName` (to ghcr.io registry path) and `newTag` (to SHA) in one command. This is the official Kustomize CLI pattern, not fragile sed.
   179→    6. **Short SHA format: `sha-$(echo '${{ github.sha }}' | cut -c1-7)`** -- matches the exact format produced by `docker/metadata-action`'s `type=sha,prefix=sha-` (7-character short SHA with `sha-` prefix).
   180→    7. **`git diff --staged --quiet && echo "No changes" && exit 0`** -- prevents empty commits if the workflow is re-run on the same commit (idempotent).
   181→    8. **`[skip ci]` in commit message** -- belt-and-suspenders protection against infinite loops. GITHUB_TOKEN already prevents re-triggering push-based workflows, but `[skip ci]` is an additional safety net.
   182→    9. **No PAT** -- the default GITHUB_TOKEN is used (no explicit token configuration needed). GitHub Actions does not trigger push-based workflows for commits made by GITHUB_TOKEN.
   183→
   184→    DO NOT modify any existing job (build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway). Only append the new job.
   185→  </action>
   186→  <verify>
   187→    <automated>cd /Users/baotoq/Work/micro-commerce && grep -q "update-manifests:" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "needs: \[build-web, build-apiservice, build-gateway\]" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "github.ref == 'refs/heads/master'" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "contents: write" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "setup-kustomize@v2" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "kustomize edit set image apiservice=ghcr.io" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "kustomize edit set image gateway=ghcr.io" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "kustomize edit set image web=ghcr.io" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "\[skip ci\]" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q "git diff --staged --quiet" .github/workflows/container-images.yml && grep -q 'cut -c1-7' .github/workflows/container-images.yml && echo "ALL CHECKS PASS"</automated>
   188→  </verify>
   189→  <done>
   190→    - update-manifests job added to container-images.yml
   191→    - Job depends on all 3 build jobs (needs clause)
   192→    - Job only runs on master branch pushes (if condition)
   193→    - Job has contents: write permission
   194→    - Setup Kustomize step (imranismail/setup-kustomize@v2) installs standalone kustomize CLI before edit commands
   195→    - kustomize edit set image updates all 3 service overlays with ghcr.io registry path + SHA tag
   196→    - git commit uses [skip ci] message
   197→    - git diff --staged --quiet prevents empty commits
   198→    - SHA format matches docker/metadata-action output (sha- prefix + 7 chars)
   199→    - No existing jobs modified
   200→  </done>
   201→</task>

    58→### 🚧 v3.0 Kubernetes & GitOps (In Progress)
    59→
    60→**Milestone Goal:** Deploy the full MicroCommerce stack to a kind-based Kubernetes cluster using GitOps via ArgoCD, with GitHub Actions CI building and pushing images to ghcr.io, Kustomize managing manifests, and Sealed Secrets handling credentials safely in Git.
    61→
    62→- [x] **Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline** - Build production-ready images for all three services and push them to ghcr.io via GitHub Actions (completed 2026-02-25)
    63→- [x] **Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets** - Deploy PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak in the kind cluster with Sealed Secrets for all credentials (completed 2026-02-26)
    64→- [x] **Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport** - Deploy ApiService, Gateway, and Web with Kustomize base/overlay structure and RabbitMQ transport support (completed 2026-02-26)
    65→- [x] **Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps** - Install ArgoCD and wire app-of-apps to manage all cluster resources from Git (completed 2026-03-02)
    66→- [ ] **Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure** - Extend CI to commit SHA image tags back to the overlay, completing the full GitOps loop
    67→- [ ] **Phase 28: Observability** - Deploy OTEL Collector and standalone Aspire Dashboard for in-cluster monitoring
    68→
    69→## Phase Details
    70→
    71→### Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline
    72→**Goal**: Three production-ready container images exist in ghcr.io and are built automatically on every push to master
    73→**Depends on**: Phase 22 (v2.0 complete)
    74→**Requirements**: CONT-01, CONT-02, CONT-03, CONT-04, CICD-01
    75→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    76→  1. `docker build` succeeds for ApiService, Gateway, and Web with no warnings or root-user violations
    77→  2. `docker run` starts each service and the process responds to health checks within 30 seconds
    78→  3. A push to master triggers the GitHub Actions workflow and all three images appear in ghcr.io tagged with the commit SHA
    79→  4. Image sizes are reasonable: .NET images use chiseled runtime and Next.js uses standalone output with node:alpine
    80→**Plans**: TBD
    81→
    82→### Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets
    83→**Goal**: PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak run stably in the kind cluster with all credentials sealed in Git
    84→**Depends on**: Phase 23
    85→**Requirements**: INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03, INFRA-04, INFRA-05, GOPS-03
    86→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    87→  1. `kind create cluster --config kind-config.yaml` creates a cluster and all infrastructure pods reach Running state
    88→  2. PostgreSQL data persists across pod restarts (PVC-backed StatefulSet)
    89→  3. Keycloak admin UI is reachable and the MicroCommerce realm is pre-loaded from a ConfigMap
    90→  4. No plaintext credentials exist in any committed YAML file; all secrets are SealedSecret objects
    91→  5. Keycloak startup probe is configured with sufficient timeout (15s delay + 60s probing window) to survive slow first-boot realm import
    92→**Plans**: 4 plans
    93→- [x] 24-01-PLAN.md -- Kind cluster config, Kustomize scaffold, PostgreSQL StatefulSet
    94→- [x] 24-02-PLAN.md -- RabbitMQ Deployment, Keycloak Deployment with realm import
    95→- [x] 24-03-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script with SealedSecrets integration
    96→- [x] 24-04-PLAN.md -- Gap closure: fix ROADMAP misalignment on success criterion #5
    97→
    98→### Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport
    99→**Goal**: ApiService, Gateway, and Web are deployed via Kustomize and communicate correctly using K8s DNS and RabbitMQ messaging
   100→**Depends on**: Phase 24
   101→**Requirements**: MFST-01, MFST-02, MFST-03, MFST-04, MFST-05, MFST-06, TRAN-01, TRAN-02
   102→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   103→  1. The storefront loads in a browser via the Gateway NodePort — browsing products and adding to cart works end-to-end
   104→  2. Checkout completes successfully using the RabbitMQ transport (saga orchestrates stock reservation and mock payment)
   105→  3. All pods report liveness and readiness as healthy (`kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce` shows all Running/Ready)
   106→  4. All resources are in the `micro-commerce` namespace and have CPU/memory resource limits set
   107→  5. Setting `MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT=AzureServiceBus` (Aspire dev path) and `RabbitMQ` (K8s path) both produce working configurations
   108→  6. ApiService startup probe prevents liveness failure during slow first-boot EF Core migration (deferred from Phase 24 INFRA-05)
   109→**Plans**: 3 plans
   110→- [x] 25-01-PLAN.md -- MassTransit RabbitMQ transport switching, health endpoint fix, conditional Azure SDK
   111→- [x] 25-02-PLAN.md -- ApiService, Gateway, Web K8s Deployment/Service manifests, Kustomize base/overlay
   112→- [x] 25-03-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script extension with app image build, kind load, and full stack deployment

   135→### Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps
   136→**Goal**: ArgoCD manages all cluster resources from Git using app-of-apps, replacing manual kubectl apply
   137→**Depends on**: Phase 25
   138→**Requirements**: GOPS-01, GOPS-02
   139→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   140→  1. ArgoCD UI is reachable in the kind cluster and shows all services as Synced and Healthy
   141→  2. Deleting a deployment manually causes ArgoCD to automatically restore it within one sync cycle
   142→  3. The app-of-apps root Application manages each service as an independent child Application
   143→**Plans**: 2 plans
   144→- [ ] 26-01-PLAN.md -- ArgoCD manifests, child Applications, Kustomize per-service restructure
   145→- [ ] 26-02-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script ArgoCD integration and GitOps handoff
   146→
   147→### Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure
   148→**Goal**: A push to master automatically flows through CI image build, Git tag commit, and ArgoCD cluster rollout without manual intervention
   149→**Depends on**: Phase 26
   150→**Requirements**: CICD-02
   151→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   152→  1. After a push to master, a new pod with the updated SHA-tagged image is running in the cluster within 5 minutes of the CI workflow completing
   153→  2. Every deployment is traceable to a Git commit — the image tag in the overlay matches the commit SHA that triggered the build
   154→**Plans**: TBD
